speaker: Joy
text: Do you ever look at someone and wonder, "What is going on inside their head?" Well, I know. ...Well, I know Riley's head.
parenthetical: (Fades up in a white space; Bill and Jill Andersen are admiring their newborn baby Riley, who wakes up and coos happily. Zoom in on Riley's head.) | (In Riley's mind, everything is black. From out of the darkness steps a glowing figure, Joy. She comes forward and sees a blank screen above her; below her is a single button. She presses it, and it turns yellow like her. Riley is heard cooing.)

speaker: Joy
text: Hmm?
parenthetical: (Zoom back from Joy to the real world, showing the baby Riley as she coos and giggles happily.)

speaker: Joy
text: And there she was...
parenthetical: (On the screen, Bill and Jill's faces materialize into view.)

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: Hello, Riley.

speaker: Mr. Andersen
text: Oh, look at you. Aren't you a little bundle of joy?
parenthetical: (A ding is heard, and a glowing yellow orb of what just happened is formed from behind the screen. Joy examines it.)

speaker: Memory Mr. Andersen
text: Aren't you a little bundle of joy?

speaker: Joy
text: Whoa.
parenthetical: (She puts the memory back in place and it rolls across a rack around the area, making the lights come up and revealing a red-violet space of gears, as Headquarters is starting to form. Joy is astounded; she presses the button again, and Riley coos in response. Joy beams.)

speaker: Joy
text: It was amazing. Just Riley and me... Forever.
parenthetical: (Riley keeps giggling and Joy smiles in reply; suddenly, Riley starts crying, which confuses her.)

speaker: Joy
text: Well, for 33 seconds.
parenthetical: (Next to Joy, a droopy and gloomy blue figure, Sadness, is working the button, which turns blue in response.)

speaker: Sadness
text: I'm Sadness.

speaker: Joy
text: Oh, hello. I... I'm Joy. So... Can I just... If you could... I just want to fix that. Thanks.
parenthetical: (The two start arguing over the button as she speaks.)

speaker: Joy
text: And that was just the beginning. Headquarters only got more crowded from there.
parenthetical: (Riley, now a toddler, runs through the house pulling a wagon and giggling. In her mind, in the now formed Headquarters, Fear, a jagged purple figure, comes out from behind joy. The single button is now replaced with a control console.)

speaker: Fear
text: Very nice. Okay, looks like you got this. Very good.
parenthetical: (The screen shows everything through Riley's eyes, as she is running.)

speaker: Fear
text: Whoa, sharp turn! NO, LOOK OUT! NO!
parenthetical: (He takes action; the console turns purple in response.)

speaker: Joy
text: That's Fear. He's really good at keeping Riley safe.
parenthetical: (Fear's actions prompt Riley to stop running and notice the plugged cable, and step over it slowly.)

speaker: Fear
text: Easy, easy. AGH! OH, MY! Oh, we're good. We're good.
parenthetical: (In Headquarters, the memories have been nestled on a shelf off to the left; a purple memory, indicating a fearful one, rolls in to nestle against the yellow one on the right.)

speaker: Joy
text: Whew!

speaker: Sadness
text: Good job.

speaker: Fear
text: Thank you. Thank you very much.

speaker: Joy
text: And we're back!
parenthetical: (She takes over, prompting Riley to resume her silliness, knocking over a lamp in the process. Mr. Andersen picks her up.)

speaker: Young Riley
text: Oh!
parenthetical: (She is put in her high chair for dinner; she is served a bowl full of broccoli as Mr. Andersen tries to feed her a stalk.)

speaker: Mr. Andersen
text: Here we go. All right, open.

speaker: Joy
text: Hmm. This looks new.

speaker: Fear
text: You think it's safe?

speaker: Sadness
text: What is it?
parenthetical: (On the screen, Mr. Andersen brings the spoonful forward.)

speaker: Mr. Andersen
text: Ahh...?
parenthetical: (Here comes a green feminine figure, Disgust, who takes control.)

speaker: Disgust
text: Okay, caution! There is a dangerous smell, people! Hold on. What is that?

speaker: Joy
text: This is Disgust. She basically keeps Riley from being poisoned. Physically and socially.

speaker: Disgust
text: That is not brightly colored or shaped like a dinosaur. Hold on, guys. It's broccoli!
parenthetical: (She retches and works the console, turning it green; this prompts Riley to react.)

speaker: Young Riley
text: YUCKY!
parenthetical: (She flips her bowl, spilling the broccoli into Mr. Andersen's face. On the memory shelf, a green memory rolls in, indicating a disgusted one.)

speaker: Disgust
text: Well, I just saved our lives.

speaker: Fear and Sadness
text: Whew!

speaker: Disgust
text: Yeah. You're welcome.

speaker: Mr. Andersen
text: Riley, if you don't eat your dinner, you're not gonna get any dessert.
parenthetical: (Shifts to a red firebrick-like figure, Anger, who sits and reads a newspaper with a headline "NO DESSERT!"; he puts it down lividly.)

speaker: Anger
text: Wait. Did he just say we couldn't have dessert?
parenthetical: (He puts down the newspaper and steps up to the console; his head begins to flame up.)

speaker: Joy
text: That's Anger. He cares very deeply about things being fair.

speaker: Anger
text: So that's how you want to play it, old man?! No dessert?! Oh, sure. We'll eat our dinner! Right after YOU eat THIS!
parenthetical: (The console turns red as he mans it, and, letting out a frustrated yell, does his thing as a heavy drum-roll is heard in the background score. This prompts Riley to scream in her chair as well, and a red angry memory rolls in on the shelf. She keeps throwing a fit when Mr. Andersen intervenes.)

speaker: Mr. Andersen
text: Riley, Riley, here comes an airplane!
parenthetical: (He makes the sound of an airplane flying; Anger immediately calms down.)

speaker: Anger
text: Oh, airplane. We got an airplane, everybody. Joy, Sadness, Disgust: Oh!

speaker: Fear
text: Airplane.
parenthetical: (Mr. Andersen successfully feeds Riley, who has also calmed down; a yellow memory appears on the shelf. Sadness then lounges next to Joy, who is obviously not pleased by her presence.)

speaker: Joy
text: And you've met Sadness. She... Well, she...
parenthetical: (Quick inter-cuts of Riley bawling like mad over various mishaps: Ripping her teddy bear, spilling her ice cream, being dragged out of the store, and being strapped in her car seat to go home. Back to Headquarters.)

speaker: Joy
text: I'm not actually sure what she does.
parenthetical: (She smiles meekly at Sadness, who promptly exits.)

speaker: Joy
text: And I've checked, there's no place for her to go, so... She's good, we're good. It's all great!
parenthetical: (She goes over to the left side of Headquarters, shelves lined with various memory orbs. Most of them are happy and yellow.)

speaker: Joy
text: Anyway, these are Riley's Memories, and they're mostly happy, you'll notice. Not to brag.
parenthetical: (She inspects one memory of Riley and her friend Meg playing on a playground slide.)

speaker: Memory Young Meg
text: Whoo-hoo!

speaker: Memory Young Meg
text: Whoo-hoo!

speaker: Joy
text: But the really important ones are over here.
parenthetical: (She pushes down a slot; this brings up a pedestal of five dazzlingly bright memory orbs arranged in a circle.)

speaker: Joy
text: I don't want to get too technical, but these are called Core Memories. Each one came from a super important time in Riley's life. Uh, like when she first scored a goal?
parenthetical: (Flashback to Riley and her parents ice skating on the lake, playing pretend hockey.)

speaker: Joy
text: Oh, that was so amazing!
parenthetical: (A stumble, she unknowingly hits the puck and scores a goal.)

speaker: Mr. Andersen
text: Hey! Would you look at that? Very nice! We got a future center here!

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: Nice job!
parenthetical: (In Headquarters, the emotions cheer in response. Suddenly, a loud ding and bright light catches them off guard. From behind the screen is a brightly glowing orb of the memory, which slips into the floor and slides through an underground track to the pedestal, which shoots out a beam of light and creates a floating island outside decorated with various hockey equipment.)

speaker: Joy
text: And each Core Memory powers a different aspect of Riley's personality. Like... Hockey Island!
parenthetical: (The emotions go to observe the five islands outside, with the hockey one in the middle. To the far left is an island of various toys and silly things, and a big clown's face.)

speaker: Joy
text: Goofball Island is my personal favorite.
parenthetical: (Flashback: Riley runs through the house naked as her parents chase her.)

speaker: Mr. Andersen
text: Ah, come back here, you little monkey!
parenthetical: (This act of silliness is what sets the island in action, showing the clown's eyes spin around and its tongue sticking out, a mallet hitting its head, and a series of practical jokes like a Ferris wheel of shoes honking a horn and a monkey centerpiece spinning plates.)

speaker: Mr. Andersen
text: Oh, you're silly!

speaker: Joy
text: Yep, Goofball is the best.
parenthetical: (Just to the left is an island showing two heads of people talking to each other among with various "friendly" material, like hearts, stars, and statuettes of people dancing.)

speaker: Joy
text: Friendship Island is pretty good too.
parenthetical: (Flashback: Riley and Meg walking arm-in-arm, leg-over-leg, down the sidewalk. To the left of Hockey Island is an island with various integrity memorabilia, such as scales, gavels, and a courthouse on a hill.)

speaker: Joy
text: Oh, I love Honesty Island. And that's the truth!
parenthetical: (Flashback: A guilty Riley stands near a broken plate and holds up a hammer admitting she was behind it. The last island to the far right features various family-oriented landmarks, such as a family tree, a board game, the Andersens' car hauling a trailer, three figures jumping on a trampoline, and a trio of gigantic statues representing the Andersens in a warm embrace.)

speaker: Joy
text: And of course, Family Island is amazing!
parenthetical: (Flashback: Riley and her parents decorate cookies together. Riley stuffs her cookie in Mr. Andersens' mouth.)

speaker: Joy
text: The point is, the Islands of Personality are what make Riley... Riley!
parenthetical: (Quick inter-cuts: Riley in the bath.)

speaker: Young Riley
text: Look out, mermaid!
parenthetical: (She makes a big splash; Riley is now in the living room crawling across the couch. In Headquarters, Joy inserts a flower chip into the console which projects a daydream from Riley's view; the floor becoming imaginary lava.)

speaker: Young Riley
text: Lava! Whee!
parenthetical: (She jumps to the chair: Now she sits and draws a doodle of a pink elephant creature on the living room wall.)

speaker: Young Riley
text: Who's your friend who likes to play? Bing Bong, Bing Bong!
parenthetical: (On a frozen pond, the six-year-old Riley skates for her parents.)

speaker: Mr. Andersen
text: Hey! Very nice!

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: Where'd you learn that?
parenthetical: (In Headquarters, Joy "skates" around the floor. On the front doorstep, the 11-year-old Riley and Meg drink Slushees; suddenly, Riley gets a brain freeze from drinking too fast.)

speaker: Riley
text: Ahhh!!!
parenthetical: (In Headquarters, everything and everyone begins to ice over.)

speaker: Fear
text: BRAIN FREEZE!!!
parenthetical: (The emotions scream as they succumb to the freezing; first Anger, then Fear, and finally Sadness, whose eyes remain the only part active once immobile. On the ice pond, Riley is now participating in a hockey game; she successfully scores as the crowd cheers.)

speaker: Riley
text: Yeah!

speaker: Joy
text: Yay!
parenthetical: (Riley is now tucked in bed; Mr. Andersen clicks off the light.)

speaker: Mr. Andersen
text: Good night, kiddo.

speaker: Riley
text: Good night, Dad.
parenthetical: (The door closes; Riley looks at the glow-in-the-dark stars on her bedroom ceiling as Joy watches. The screen dims out like a TV switching off as Riley falls asleep.)

speaker: Joy
text: And... we're out. That's what I'm talking about! Whoo! Another perfect day! Nice job, everybody! Let's get those memories down to Long Term.
parenthetical: (Joy presses a button on the floor with her foot; the memories begin to slide off the shelves and across roof trails.)

speaker: Fear
text: All right, we did not die today! I call that an unqualified success.
parenthetical: (Disgust observes as the memories continue to pachinko and flush out, then sink into the floor through the underground trails as the recall tube in the roof covers the hole in the floor and sucks the memories up right through it. Joy goes to the window and watches the memories rain down across Long Term Memory like falling stars.)

speaker: Joy
text: And that's it. We love our girl. She's got great friends and a great house. Things couldn't be better. After all, Riley's 11 now. What could happen?

speaker: Joy
text: What...? Sadness, Anger, Fear, Disgust: AAUGH!!! Sadness, Anger, Fear, Disgust: AAUGH!!!
parenthetical: (Mr. Andersen closes the trunk of the car.) | (A moving van drives off.)

speaker: Joy
text: Okay, not what I had in mind. Sadness, Anger, Fear, Disgust: AAUGH!!!
parenthetical: (MUSIC PLAYING)

speaker: JOY
text: Hey, look! The Golden Gate Bridge! Isn't that great? It's not made out of solid gold like we thought, which is kind of disappointing, but still...

speaker: Fear
text: : Hey, I sure am glad you told me earthquakes are a myth, Joy. Otherwise I'd be terrified right now.

speaker: Joy
text: Ah... yeah.

speaker: Anger
text: These are my kind of people.

speaker: Mr. Andersen
text: Alright, just a few more blocks. We're almost to our new house.

speaker: Anger
text: Step on it, Daddy.

speaker: Disgust
text: Why don't we just live in the smelly car? We're already been in it forever!

speaker: Joy
text: Which... actually was really lucky, because that gave us plenty of time to think about what our new house is going to look like. What? Let's review the top 5 daydreams. - Fear: Woo, that looks safe! - Sadness: That looks nice.

speaker: Joy
text: Ooh, this'll be great for Riley. Oh, no, no, no, no, no... this one!

speaker: Disgust
text: Joy, for the last time, she cannot live in a cookie.

speaker: Disgust
text: That's the one, it comes with a dragon.

speaker: Joy
text: We're getting close, I can feel it. Here it is. Here's our new house. And...
parenthetical: (We see the house.)

speaker: Joy
text: Maybe it's nice on the inside.

speaker: Anger
text: We're suppose to live here?

speaker: Sadness
text: Do we have to?

speaker: Disgust
text: I'm telling you, it smells like something died in here.

speaker: Fear
text: Can you die from moving?

speaker: Joy
text: Guys, you're overreacting. Nobody is dying.

speaker: Disgust
text: A dead mouse!

speaker: Fear
text: AAAAAH!!!

speaker: Anger
text: Great!

speaker: Disgust
text: I'm gonna be sick.

speaker: Fear
text: It's a house of the dead! What're we gonna do? We're gonna get rabies!

speaker: Anger
text: Get off of me!

speaker: Fear
text: Waahh!

speaker: Joy
text: Hey, hey, all through the drive, Dad talked about how cool our new room is. Let's go check it out.

speaker: Anger
text: It's gonna be great!

speaker: Fear
text: Yes, yes, yes, yes, yes!
parenthetical: (Cut to the empty room)

speaker: Fear
text: No, no, no, no, no.

speaker: Disgust
text: I'm starting to envy the dead mouse.

speaker: Anger
text: Get out the rubber ball. We're in solitary confinement.

speaker: Sadness
text: Riley can't live here.

speaker: Anger
text: She's right. This house stinks.

speaker: Fear
text: it's really bad. ???. Oh, yeah.

speaker: Disgust
text: Ugh. It's absolutely the worst. It's the worst place I've been in my entire life.

speaker: Joy
text: Hey, it's nothing our butterfly curtains couldn't fix. I read somewhere that an empty room is an opportunity.

speaker: Anger
text: Where did you read that?

speaker: Joy
text: It doesn't matter. I read it and it's great. We'll put the bed there. And the desk over there.

speaker: Fear
text: The hockey lamp goes there.

speaker: Anger
text: Uh, Put the chair there. There, there.

speaker: Disgust
text: Oh, the trophy collections.

speaker: Fear
text: Stars! Yeah, I like that.

speaker: Joy
text: Now, we're talking! Let's go get our stuff from the moving van.

speaker: Mr. Andersen
text: Alright. Goodbye. Well, guess what? The moving van won't be here until Thursday.

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: You're kidding.

speaker: Fear
text: The van is lost? It's the worst day ever!

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: You said it would be here yesterday.

speaker: Mr. Andersen
text: I know that's what I said. That's what they told me.

speaker: Fear
text: Mom and Dad are stressed out! What're we gonna do?

speaker: Anger
text: They're stressed out.

speaker: Disgust
text: ???. What is their problem?

speaker: Joy
text: I've got a great idea!

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: Did you even read the contract?

speaker: Riley
text: Andersen makes her move. She's closing in.

speaker: Mr. Andersen
text: Hey! Oh, no, you're not.

speaker: Riley
text: She's lining up for the shot. She shoots, and she scores! Coming behind you, watch out.

speaker: Riley
text: Yeah!
parenthetical: (The Emotions cheering.)

speaker: Riley
text: Come on, Grandma!

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: Ha! Grandma?!

speaker: Joy
text: Uh-oh, she put her hair up. We're in for it.

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: Heyyy, put me down!

speaker: Mr. Andersen
text: Ah... Sorry, hold on. Hello.

speaker: Joy
text: Wait, wha...?

speaker: Mr. Andersen
text: You're kidding me! Alright, stall for me, I'll be right there. The investor's supposed to show up on Thursday, not today. I gotta go.

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: It's OK, we get it.

speaker: Mr. Andersen
text: You're the best, thanks, hon. See you, sweetie.

speaker: Fear
text: Dad just left us.

speaker: Sadness
text: Oh, he doesn't love us anymore. That's sad. I should drive, right? Joy, what are you doing?

speaker: Joy
text: Ah, just give me one second. You know what I've realized? Riley hasn't had lunch. Remember?

speaker: Riley
text: Hey, I saw a pizza place down the street, maybe we can try that.

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: Pizza sounds delicious.

speaker: Fear
text: Pizza? Let's get it.

speaker: Disgust
text: Yes. Pizza.

speaker: Anger
text: Pizza. Great idea.
parenthetical: (Then the pizza vendor prepared a pizza with broccoli and offers one slice to them.)

speaker: Fear
text: What the heck is that?

speaker: Joy
text: Who puts broccoli on pizza?

speaker: Disgust
text: That's it, I'm done.

speaker: Anger
text: Congratulations San Francisco, you've ruined pizza! First the Hawaiians, and now you!

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: What kind of a pizza place only serves one kind of pizza? Must be a San Francisco thing. Huh? Still, it's not as bad as the soup. That diner in Nebraska?

speaker: Riley
text: Oh, yeah. The spoon stood up in the soup by itself. That's disgusting.

speaker: Joy
text: Oh, good. Family's running.

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: The drive out was pretty fun, huh? What was your favorite part?

speaker: Anger
text: Spitting out of the car window.

speaker: Disgust
text: Definitely not when Dad was singing.

speaker: Fear
text: Wearing a seat belt.

speaker: Joy
text: Oh, what about the time with the dinosaur?

speaker: Fear
text: Oh, that's the one!

speaker: Anger
text: Oh. For sure.

speaker: Disgust
text: Definitely.

speaker: Mr. Andersen
text: Say cheese.

speaker: Riley
text: Dad. Dad, Dad, Dad, look at the car. Just look behind you.

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: Honey.

speaker: Mr. Andersen
text: No-no-no... Ohh!

speaker: Fear
text: Nice one, Joy.

speaker: Riley
text: I like that time with the dinosaur. That was pretty funny.
parenthetical: (All of a sudden, the memory onscreen turns blue; Riley's smile fades.)

speaker: Joy
text: Wait, what. What happened?

speaker: Fear
text: She did something to the memory.

speaker: Joy
text: What did you do?

speaker: Sadness
text: I, I just touched it.

speaker: Joy
text: That shouldn't make it change.

speaker: Fear
text: Oh, change it back, Joy.

speaker: Joy
text: I'm trying.

speaker: Anger
text: You can't change it back?

speaker: Joy
text: No, I guess I can't.

speaker: Disgust
text: Good going, Sadness! Now when Riley thinks of that moment with Dad, she's gonna feel sad. Bravo!

speaker: Sadness
text: I'm sorry, Joy. I don't really know... I thought maybe if you, if you, I mean...

speaker: Disgust
text: Joy, we've got a stairway coming up.

speaker: Joy
text: Just don't touch any other memories until we figure out what's going on.

speaker: Sadness
text: OK.

speaker: Joy
text: Alright. Get ready. This is a monster railing and we are riding it all the way down.
parenthetical: (Riley sits on the railing, ready to slide.) | (Riley goes to slide... but suddenly hops off and walks down instead.)

speaker: Joy
text: Wait, what? What happened?

speaker: Fear
text: A core memory!

speaker: Joy
text: Oh, no!
parenthetical: (Cut to Sadness who looks out the window and we see Goofball Island turn off.)

speaker: Joy
text: Sadness, what're you doing?

speaker: Sadness
text: It looked like one was crooked, so I opened it and then it fell out!
parenthetical: (Riley jumps back on the railing and slides down.)

speaker: Riley
text: Woo-hoo!

speaker: Sadness
text: It's just that, I wanted to maybe hold one.

speaker: Fear
text: Woah! Joy!

speaker: Joy
text: Woah, Woah, Woah! Sadness, you nearly touched a core memory! And when you touch them, we can't change them back.

speaker: Sadness
text: I know. I'm sorry. Something's wrong with me. It's like I'm having a break down.

speaker: Joy
text: You're not having a break down. It's stress.

speaker: Sadness
text: I keep making mistakes like that. I'm awful.

speaker: Joy
text: No, you're not!

speaker: Sadness
text: And annoying.

speaker: Joy
text: The... well... Uh, you know what? You can't focus on what's going wrong. There's always a way to turn things around. To find the fun.

speaker: Sadness
text: Yeah, find the fun. I don't know how to do that.

speaker: Joy
text: OK, well... Try to think of something funny.

speaker: Sadness
text: Oh, remember the funny movie where the dog dies?

speaker: Joy
text: Oh, yeah... That's not... What about that time, with Meg? When Riley laughed so hard, milk came out of her nose. Come on!

speaker: Sadness
text: Yeah, that hurt. It felt like fire. It was...

speaker: Joy
text: OK, OK. Don't think of that. Let's try something else. Uh, what are your favorite things to do?

speaker: Sadness
text: My favorite? Um, well I like it when we're outside.

speaker: Joy
text: That's good! Like, there's the beach and sunshine. Oh, like that time we buried Dad in the sand up to his neck.

speaker: Sadness
text: I was thinking more like rain.

speaker: Joy
text: Rain. Rain is my favorite too! We can stomp around in puddles You know, there's cool umbrellas, lightning storms.

speaker: Sadness
text: More like when the rain runs down our back, and makes our shoes soggy. And we get all cold, shivery and everything just starts feeling droopy.

speaker: Joy
text: Oh, hey, hey! Easy. Why are you crying? Th-this is really the opposite of what we're going for here.

speaker: Sadness
text: Crying helps me slow down and obsess over the weight of life's problems.

speaker: Joy
text: You know what? Let's uh, think about something else. How about we read some mind manuals. Huh? Sounds fun!

speaker: Sadness
text: I've read most of them.

speaker: Joy
text: Well have you read this one? This seem interesting. Long Term Memory Retrieval. Volume

speaker: Sadness
text: No.

speaker: Joy
text: Ooh, a real page turner!

speaker: Sadness
text: Long term memory data selection via channel sub-grouping.

speaker: Joy
text: See? Fun already! Oh, you lucky dog. You're reading these cool things; I gotta go work. Life is so unfair. Ugh! Advertisement

speaker: Mr. Andersen
text: What can we do? We only got capital to last a month, maybe two. If we can't find investors by then, we're gonna have to lay people off.

speaker: Riley
text: Mom, Dad, come kiss me good night!

speaker: Mr. Andersen
text: I know. We got to land this, OK.

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: Be right there!

speaker: Fear
text: Did you hear Dad? He sounded really upset.

speaker: Fear
text: What was that? Was it a bear? It's a bear!

speaker: Disgust
text: There are no bears in San Francisco.

speaker: Anger
text: I saw a really hairy guy. He looked like a bear.

speaker: Fear
text: Oh. I'm so jumpy, my nerves are shot!

speaker: Disgust
text: Ew, I don't wanna hear about your nerves!

speaker: Anger
text: I'll tell you what it is. This move has been a bust.

speaker: Fear
text: That's what I've been telling you guys. There are at least 37 things for Riley to be scared of right now.

speaker: Disgust
text: This smell alone is enough to make her gag.

speaker: Anger
text: I can't believe Mom and Dad moved us here.

speaker: Joy
text: Look, I get it. You guys have concerns. But we've been through worse. Tell you what. Let's make a list of all the things Riley should be happy about.

speaker: Anger
text: Fine. Let's see, this house stinks, our room stinks.

speaker: Disgust
text: Pizza is weird here.

speaker: Sadness
text: Our friends are back home.

speaker: Fear
text: And all of our stuff is in the missing van.

speaker: Joy
text: Oh, come on, it could be worse.

speaker: Disgust
text: Yeah Joy. We could be lying on the dirty floor, in a bag.

speaker: Joy
text: OK, I admit it. We had a rough start. But think of all the good things that-

speaker: Anger
text: No, Joy. There's absolutely no reason for Riley to be happy right now. Let us handle this

speaker: Fear
text: I say we skip school tomorrow and lock ourselves in the bedroom.

speaker: Disgust
text: We have no clean clothes. I mean no one should see us.

speaker: Sadness
text: Yeah. We cou-could cry until we can't breathe.

speaker: Anger
text: We should lock the door and scream that curse word we know. It's a good one.

speaker: Joy
text: Now, hold on! Look, we all have our off days, you know...

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: Hi, honey.

speaker: Anger
text: The Mom-bad-news train is pulling in. Toot-toot!

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: Still no moving van, huh? Now they're saying it won't be here till Tuesday. Can you believe it?

speaker: Anger
text: Toot-toot-toot!

speaker: Riley
text: Where's Dad?

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: On the phone. This new venture is keeping him pretty busy.

speaker: Anger
text: I rest my case!

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: Your dad's a little stressed about getting his new company up and running...

speaker: Anger
text: Now, for a few well placed withering scowls.

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: I guess all I really want to say is... Thank you.

speaker: Anger
text: Huh?

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: You know, through all this confusion, you've stayed... Well, you've stayed our happy girl. Your Dad's under a lot of pressure. But, if you and I can keep smiling, it would be a big help. We can do that for him, right?

speaker: Joy
text: Woah. Well...

speaker: Riley
text: Yeah. Sure.

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: What did we do to deserve you? Sweet dreams.

speaker: Riley
text: Good night.

speaker: Anger
text: Well, you can't argue with Mom. Happy it is!

speaker: Disgust
text: Can't argue with Mom, Let's do this. Totally behind you, Joy.

speaker: Fear
text: Team Happy. Sounds great.

speaker: Joy
text: Looks like we're going into R.E.M. I got dream duty so I'll take care of sending these to long term. Great day today, guys. All is well, team Happy.

speaker: Fear
text: I love being on a team. ???

speaker: Sadness
text: Yeah. ???.

speaker: Joy
text: Alright, what's on tonight, Dream Production?
parenthetical: (In the dream, the Andersens' car flies through the sky.)

speaker: Dream Mr. Andersen
text: Well, this is it. The new place.

speaker: Dream Mouse
text: Come live with me, Riley... Eurk!

speaker: Dream Bear
text: Somebody order a broccoli pizza?

speaker: Dream Pizza
text: Eat me, I'm organic.
parenthetical: (Riley begins to cringe in her sleep.)

speaker: Joy
text: Oh, no! Who is in charge of programming down there? I know I'm not supposed to do this, but...
parenthetical: (She pushes a button on the bottom of the console, shutting off the dream.)

speaker: Joy
text: We are not going to end the day like this.

speaker: Joy
text: Don't you worry. I'm going to make sure that tomorrow is another great day. I promise. Advertisement

speaker: Joy
text: Hello, did I wake you?

speaker: Anger
text: Do you have to play that?!

speaker: Joy
text: Well, I have to practice. And I don't think of it as playing so much as hugging.
parenthetical: (They head downstairs.)

speaker: Joy
text: OK, first day of school. Very, very exciting. I was up late last night figuring out a new plan. Here it is: Fear!

speaker: Fear
text: Huh?

speaker: Joy
text: I need a list of all the possible negative outcomes on the first day at a new school.

speaker: Fear
text: Way ahead of you there. Does anyone know how to spell meteor?

speaker: Joy
text: Disgust, make sure Riley stands out today. But, also blends in.

speaker: Disgust
text: When I'm through, Riley will look so good, the other kids will look at their own outfits and barf.

speaker: Joy
text: Joy! "Yes, Joy?" You'll be in charge of the console. Keeping Riley happy all day long. And may I add, I love your dress, it's adorable. "Oh, this old thing. Thank you so much." I love the way it twirls.
parenthetical: (Outside, a train's horn is heard as it pulls in.)

speaker: Joy
text: Train of Thought, right on schedule. Anger, unload the daydreams. I ordered extra, in case things get slow in class.

speaker: Anger
text: Might come in handy. If this new school is full of boring, useless classes. Which it probably will be.

speaker: Joy
text: Oh, Sadness. I have a super important job just for you.

speaker: Sadness
text: Really?

speaker: Joy
text: M-hm, follow me.
parenthetical: (Somewhere else, she draws a chalk circle around Sadness' feet.)

speaker: Sadness
text: What are you doing?

speaker: Joy
text: And there. Perfect. This is the circle of sadness. Your job is to make sure that all the sadness stays inside of it.

speaker: Sadness
text: So, you want me just to stand here?

speaker: Joy
text: Hey, it's not my place to tell you how to do your job. Just make sure that all...the sadness stays in the circle. See? You're a pro at this! Isn't this fun?

speaker: Sadness
text: No.

speaker: Joy
text: Atta girl.
parenthetical: (She walks back to the front of the room.)

speaker: Joy
text: Alright everyone, fresh start. We are gonna have a good day, which will turn into a good week, which will turn into a good year, which turns into a good life!
parenthetical: (Riley heads for the door.)

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: So, big day. New school, new friends, huh?

speaker: Riley
text: I know. I'm kind of nervous but I'm mostly excited. How do I look?
parenthetical: (In Headquarters, Joy watches Family Island start up out the window.)

speaker: Riley
text: Do you like my purse?

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: Very cute! You gonna be Okay? You want us to walk with you?

speaker: Disgust
text: Mom and Dad, with us in public? No, thank you.

speaker: Joy
text: Yup, I'm on it.
parenthetical: (She pushes a button and Riley picks up.)

speaker: Riley
text: Nope, I'm fine. Bye Mom. Bye Dad.

speaker: Mr. Andersen
text: Have a good day at school, monkey.
parenthetical: (They make monkey noises at each other; Goofball Island sets off. Riley giggles and exits.)

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: Have a great day, sweetheart.
parenthetical: (Outside, Riley walks down the sidewalk until she comes to her new school; she stops and hesitates.)

speaker: Fear
text: Are you sure we wanna do this?

speaker: Joy
text: In we go!
parenthetical: (This prompts Riley to go right for it.)

speaker: Fear
text: OK, going in. Yes!
parenthetical: (Riley sits in her new classroom; students talk amongst each other. Riley notices them; Disgust picks up three girls in particular.)

speaker: Disgust
text: OK. We got a group of cool girls at two o'clock.

speaker: Joy
text: How do you know?

speaker: Disgust
text: Double ears pierced, infinity scarf.
parenthetical: (Their leader turns around.)

speaker: Joy
text: Woah! Is she wearing eyeshadow?

speaker: Disgust
text: Yeah, we want to be friends with them.

speaker: Joy
text: Let's go talk to them!

speaker: Disgust
text: Are you kidding?! We're not talking to them! We want them to like us.

speaker: Joy
text: Oh, yeah. Wait, what?
parenthetical: (Fear comes over holding a stack of sheets.)

speaker: Fear
text: Almost finished with the potential disasters. Worst scenario is either quicksand, spontaneous combustion, or getting called on by the teacher. So as long as none of this happens...
parenthetical: (The teacher arrives and steps to the front of the room.)

speaker: Teacher
text: OK, everybody, we have a new student in class today.

speaker: Fear
text: Are you kidding me?! Out of the gate! This is not happening!

speaker: Teacher
text: Riley, would you like to tell us something about yourself?

speaker: Fear
text: NOOO! Pretend we can't speak English!

speaker: Joy
text: Don't worry, I got this.
parenthetical: (She does her work; this prompts Riley to speak up.)

speaker: Riley
text: Um, okay... My name is Riley Andersen. I'm from Minnesota and now I live here.
parenthetical: (She glances over at some of the students and the cool girl.)

speaker: Teacher
text: And how about Minnesota? Can you tell us something about it? Well, you certainly get a lot more snow than we do.

speaker: Joy
text: She's hilarious...!
parenthetical: (She does more work.)

speaker: Riley
text: Yeah, it gets pretty cold. The lake freezes over and that’s when we play hockey. I'm on a great team. We're called the Prairie Dogs. My friend, Meg, plays forward, and my dad's the coach.
parenthetical: (Joy pushes a button which makes a happy memory comes down the recall tube and projects onto the screen; it shows the Andersens playing hockey on a frozen pond.)

speaker: Riley
text: Pretty much everyone in my family skates. It's kind of a family tradition. We go out on the lake almost every weekend.
parenthetical: (Suddenly, the memory turns blue; Riley's mood sinks.)

speaker: Riley
text: Or, we did, till... I moved away.

speaker: Disgust
text: Hey. What gives?

speaker: Fear
text: Wait, what?
parenthetical: (The culprit is Sadness, who has left her circle and touched the memory, changing it.)

speaker: Joy
text: Hey! Sadness?! You touched a memory?! We talked about this!

speaker: Sadness
text: Oh, yeah, I know. I'm sorry.

speaker: Joy
text: Get back in your circle!
parenthetical: (She attempts to cheer Riley, but the console does not respond.)

speaker: Joy
text: Ugh... What's going on?!

speaker: Fear
text: Get it out of there, Joy!

speaker: Joy
text: Eject!!
parenthetical: (Still nothing happens; Riley's homesickness begins to take over.)

speaker: Riley
text: We... used to... play tag, and stuff...

speaker: Disgust
text: Cool kids whispering at three o'clock!
parenthetical: (Indeed the cool girls are whispering to each other.)

speaker: Fear
text: Did you see that look?

speaker: Joy
text: Oh, no.

speaker: Fear
text: They're judging us!
parenthetical: (Joy and the emotions struggle to remove the memory from the recall unit; behind them, Sadness notices the console is open and rushes toward it...)

speaker: Joy
text: Somebody help, grab that thing... Everybody put in...

speaker: Riley
text: But, everything's different now... Since we moved...
parenthetical: (She starts shedding tears and sniffs again; the students stare at her in silence.)

speaker: Fear
text: Oh, no. WE'RE CRYING! AT SCHOOL!!

speaker: Joy
parenthetical: (Still struggling, Joy sees Sadness is working the console, it responding to her and her alone.)

speaker: Joy
text: Sadness, what are you doing?!
parenthetical: (She successfully ejects the memory and rushes up to her, pulling her away from the console.)

speaker: Sadness
text: Whoa! I'm sorry. I, oh...oh!
parenthetical: (A loud flash and ding is heard; a new core memory appears from behind the screen. Unlike the others, this one is blue and sad.)

speaker: Fear
text: It's a core memory.

speaker: Disgust
text: But it's blue.
parenthetical: (Indeed it is; it shows Riley crying in front of the whole class due to her homesickness.)

speaker: Joy
text: No, wait! Stop it! No! Agh! No!
parenthetical: (The memory sinks into the floor before she could grab it; it flows down through the track to the core memory pedestal, and she opens the pedestal and grabs the memory just before it could settle in. She then pushes the "end of day memory flush" button to activate the recall tube and flush all the memories off the shelves.)

speaker: Sadness
text: Joy, no. That's a core memory! ...Wait!

speaker: Joy
text: Hey! Stop it! Let go!
parenthetical: (She breaks free of her grasp; she stumbles backward and hits the pedestal, causing all the core memories to fall out, and the pedestal powers down as they gasp.)

speaker: Fear
text: The core memories!
parenthetical: (The result causes the light poles to go out; the core memories power down and become greyscale. Riley's head raises, still breaking down and losing thought.)

speaker: Joy
text: AGH!
parenthetical: (She throws the sad core memory aside and gathers up all the happy ones that fell out. But then she sees Sadness trying to insert the sad core memory and gasps; she jumps at Sadness and she loses grip, dropping the sad core memory as it rolls toward the activated recall tube and gets sucked up through it and out of the roof to Long Term Memory. But it is still not done yet; one of the happy core memories begins rolling toward the still-sucking tube as well as it starts coming down from the ceiling.)

speaker: Joy
text: NO, NO, NO, NO!
parenthetical: (She grabs the memory but gets sucked up the tube herself; she tries to keep herself weighed down by keeping her foot hooked outside; she struggles to reach her other foot down, but Sadness gets sucked toward her as well!)

speaker: Sadness
text: Whoa... Joy, Sadness: AAAAGH!!
parenthetical: (Both Joy and Sadness are sucked up!) | (They disappear out the roof hatch, and the door closes as the tube retracts. Riley silently sits down and wipes her tears.)

speaker: Teacher
text: Thank you, Riley. I know it can be tough moving to a new place. But we're happy to have you here. Alright everyone, get out your history books, and turn to chapter
parenthetical: (The students do so; Riley hides her face behind her book, not saying a word. In Headquarters, the remaining emotions are still distraught.)

speaker: Anger
text: Can I say that curse word now?

speaker: JOY
text: Oh, no... One, two, three... okay, got ’em. Wha-- where are we? Joy takes in the shelving around her.

speaker: JOY
text: Long-Term Memory...!
parenthetical: (Joy grabs the core memories and jumps out of the dumpster. She sees GOOFBALL ISLAND, silent and dark.)

speaker: JOY
text: Goofball Island?
parenthetical: (Joy surveys the islands beyond. They’re all dark.)

speaker: SADNESS
text: Riley’s Islands of Personality. They’re ALL down! This is bad.

speaker: JOY
text: We-- we can fix this. We just have to get back to Headquarters, plug the core memories in, and Riley will be back to normal.
parenthetical: (Joy and Sadness hurry towards headquarters.)

speaker: SADNESS
text: Oh. Riley has no core memories, no personality islands and no--

speaker: JOY
text: Wha-- What is it?

speaker: SADNESS
text: You! You're not in headquarters. Without you, Riley can’t be happy. We gotta get you back up there.

speaker: JOY
text: I’m coming, Riley.
parenthetical: (They set off across the bridge to Goofball Island. We see the LIGHTLINE beyond it, leading towards Headquarters.)

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: So, as it turns out, the green trash can is not recycling. It's for greens, like compost and eggshells. And the blue one is recycling, and the black one...

speaker: Disgust
text: Riley is acting so weird! Why is she acting so weird?

speaker: Anger
text: What do you expect? All the Islands are down!

speaker: Disgust
text: Joy would know what to do.

speaker: Fear
text: That's it! Until she gets back, we just do what Joy would do.

speaker: Disgust
text: Good idea! Anger, Fear, Disgust... How are we supposed to be happy?

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: Hey, Riley, I got good news. I found a Junior Hockey League, right here in San Francisco. And get this! Tryouts are tomorrow after school. What luck, right?

speaker: Anger
text: Hockey?

speaker: Disgust
text: Uh-oh, what do we do?

speaker: Fear
text: Guys, this... Here! You-You pretend to be Joy.

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: Wouldn't it be great to be back out on the ice?
parenthetical: (Disgust controls the command which it turns green.)

speaker: Riley
text: Oh, yeah, that sounds fantastic.

speaker: Fear
text: What was that? That wasn't anything like Joy!

speaker: Disgust
text: Uh, because I'm NOT Joy!

speaker: Fear
text: Yeah, no kidding!

speaker: Mom's Sadness
text: Did you guys pick up on that?

speaker: Mom's Joy
text: Sure did.

speaker: Mom's Disgust
text: Mm-hmm. something's wrong.

speaker: Mom's Anger
text: Should we ask her?

speaker: Mom's Sadness
text: Let's probe. But keep it subtle so she doesn't notice.

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: So, how was the first day of school?

speaker: Anger
text: She's probing us.

speaker: Disgust
text: I'm done. You pretend to be Joy.

speaker: Fear
text: What? OK. Um...
parenthetical: (Fear takes control of the command as it turns purple.)

speaker: Riley
text: It was fine, I guess, I dunno.

speaker: Disgust
text: Oh, very smooth. That was just like Joy!
parenthetical: (Fear puts his puppy eyes after realizing he is not Joy.)

speaker: Mom's Anger
text: Something is definitely going on!

speaker: Mom's Disgust
text: She's never acted like this before. What should we do?

speaker: Mom's Sadness
text: We're gonna find out what's happening. But we'll need support. Signal the husband.

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: Ahem!
parenthetical: (In Mr. Andersen's mind, his emotions are watching a hockey game.)

speaker: Hockey Announcer
text: With a nice pass, over to Reed, coming across center-

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: Ahem!

speaker: Dad's Anger
text: Uh-oh. She's looking at us. Uh, what did she say?
parenthetical: (Dad's anger turned off the hockey game to pay attention to Mrs. Andersen.)

speaker: Dad's Fear
text: What? Oh, uh, sorry, sir, no one was listening.

speaker: Dad's Anger
text: Is it garbage night? Uh, we left the toilet seat up? What? What is it, woman? What?
parenthetical: (Mom's emotions look at Mr. Andersen's face as everyone except Mom's sadness get mad about it.)

speaker: Mom's Disgust
text: Ugh. He's making that stupid face again.

speaker: Mom's Anger
text: I could strangle him right now!

speaker: Mom's Sadness
text: Signal him again.
parenthetical: (Mrs. Andersen signals her husband to talk to Riley.)

speaker: Mr. Andersen
text: Ah! So... Riley, how was school?

speaker: Mom's Joy
text: Seriously?

speaker: Mom's Fear
text: Why does he do this all the time?

speaker: Mom's Disgust
text: Please.

speaker: Mom's Sadness
text: You gotta be kidding me!

speaker: Mom's Anger
text: For this, we gave up that Brazilian helicopter pilot?!

speaker: Anger
text: Move! I'll be Joy.
parenthetical: (Anger takes control which the command turns red)

speaker: Riley
text: School was great, alright?

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: Riley? Is everything OK?

speaker: Riley
text: Argh...

speaker: Dad's Fear
text: Sir, she just rolled her eyes at us.

speaker: Dad's Anger
text: What is her deal? Alright, make a show of force. I don't wanna have to put the foot down.

speaker: Dad's Fear
text: No, not the foot!

speaker: Mr. Andersen
text: Riley, I do not like this new attitude.

speaker: Anger
text: Oh, I'll show you attitude, old man!

speaker: Fear
text: No, no-no-no, stay happy.
parenthetical: (Anger punches Fear away and takes control.)

speaker: Riley
text: What is your problem? Just leave me alone!

speaker: Dad's Fear
text: Sir, reporting high levels of sass.

speaker: Dad's Anger
text: Take it to DefCon

speaker: Dad's Fear
text: Your heard that gentlemen. DefCon 2!

speaker: Mr. Andersen
text: Listen, young lady, I don't know where this disrespectful attitude came from...

speaker: Anger
text: You want a piece of this, pops? Come and get it!!

speaker: Riley
text: Yeah? Well, well...

speaker: Dad's Anger
text: Here it comes. Prepare the foot!

speaker: Dad's Fear
text: Keys to Safety Position! Ready to launch on your command, sir.
parenthetical: (Dad's Fear places his hand near the button but doesn't press it yet. Mr. Andersen looks firmly at Riley. Anger pulls the levers and yells.)

speaker: Anger
text: AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAARRRRRRRRRRRRGGGGGGHHHHHHHH!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

speaker: Riley
text: JUST SHUT UP!
parenthetical: (Riley bangs on the table in anger much to her father's disappointment and anger.)

speaker: Dad's Anger
text: FIRE!
parenthetical: (Dad's Fear presses the button.)

speaker: Mr. Andersen
text: That's it! Go to your room! Now!

speaker: Riley
text: Hah! Gah!
parenthetical: (Riley storms off the table and walks to her room angrily.)

speaker: Dad's Fear
text: Foot is down. The foot is down! Woo!

speaker: Dad's Anger
text: Good job, gentlemen. That could've been a disaster.

speaker: Mom's Sadness
text: Well, that was a disaster.

speaker: Brazilian Helicopter Pilot
text: Come, fly with me, gatinha.

speaker: SADNESS
text: We’re gonna walk out there? On that?

speaker: JOY
text: It’s the quickest way back.

speaker: SADNESS
text: But it’s right over the Memory Dump. If we fall, we’ll be forgotten forever!

speaker: JOY
text: We have to do this. For Riley. Just follow my footsteps.

speaker: SADNESS
text: Ohh... ok.

speaker: JOY
text: It's not that high. It’s totally fiii... Whoa...
parenthetical: (She loses her balance. Almost drops a memory over the side. Sadness steps out onto the light-line. They inch along.) | (LATER Dad knocks on Riley’s door, peeks in.)

speaker: DAD
text: Hey.
parenthetical: (Riley’s in her sleeping bag. She doesn’t answer.)

speaker: DAD
text: So uh, things got a little out of hand downstairs. You want to talk about it? Come on. Where’s my happy girl? Monkey.

speaker: Anger
text: Oh! He’s trying to start up Goofball. DAD Come on.
parenthetical: (Fear looks at the empty Core Memory Holder, then out the window: GOOFBALL ISLAND is dark and silent.) | (Riley looks at Dad, but TURNS AWAY.) | (Goofball Island crumbles and breaks. Joy sees the light-line breaking ahead.)

speaker: JOY
text: Ahhh! Go back! RUN! RUN! RUN!
parenthetical: (They run back onto Goofball Island as the light-line crumbles. Joy and Sadness frantically outrun collapsing debris. They leap across the buckling bridge, making it over to the cliff just in time to see Goofball Island fall into the dump.)

speaker: JOY
text: What-- ?
parenthetical: (Joy watches it sink.)

speaker: DAD
text: I get it, you need some alone time. We’ll talk later.
parenthetical: (The Emotions stare out the back window in disbelief.)

speaker: DISGUST
text: We have a major problem.

speaker: FEAR
text: Oh, Joy where are you?
parenthetical: (Joy watches, still in shock.)

speaker: Sadness
text: Oh, we lost Goofball Island. That means she can lose Friendship, and Hockey, and Honesty, and Family...! You can fix this, right, Joy?

speaker: Joy
text: I-I...ah... I don't know. But we have to try. OK, come on. - Riley's gone to sleep. Which is a good thing. When you think about it, because nothing else bad can happen while she's asleep. We'll be back to Headquarters before she wakes up. We'll just go across Friendship Island.
parenthetical: (Shelves jut out over the cliff edge, making Friendship Island inaccessible.)

speaker: SADNESS
text: Oh, We’ll never make it, oh... nooo...

speaker: JOY
text: No, no, no, no-no-no-no. D-don’t obsess over the weight of life’s problems, remember the funny movie where the dog dies?!
parenthetical: (Sadness face plants.)

speaker: JOY
text: Uhhh, Sadness, we don’t have time for this.
parenthetical: (She looks to her side. Memory shelves wind into the distance. Joy heads off into the maze.) | [CONT’D]

speaker: JOY
text: We’ll just have to go around! Take the scenic route.
parenthetical: [CONT’D]

speaker: SADNESS
text: Wait! Joy, you could get lost in there!

speaker: JOY
text: Think positive!

speaker: SADNESS
text: Okay. I’m positive you will get lost in there. That’s Long-Term Memory. An endless warren of corridors and shelves. I read about it in the manuals.

speaker: JOY
text: The manuals? The manuals! You read the manuals!

speaker: SADNESS
text: Yeah...

speaker: JOY
text: So you know the way back to headquarters!

speaker: SADNESS
text: I guess...

speaker: JOY
text: You are my map! Let’s go! Lead on, Mind Map! Show me where we’re going!

speaker: SADNESS
text: Okay! Only, I’m too sad to walk. Just give me a few... hours.
parenthetical: (Joy grabs Sadness’ leg and DRAGS her into the shelves.)

speaker: JOY
text: Which way? Left?

speaker: SADNESS
text: Right. No. I mean, go left. I said left was right, like “correct.”

speaker: JOY
text: Okay.

speaker: SADNESS
text: This actually feels kind of nice.
parenthetical: (Joy heads off into the COMPLICATED LABYRINTH ahead of them.)

speaker: JOY
text: Okay! Here we go. We’ll be back to Headquarters before morning. We can do it. This’ll be easy. This is working!

speaker: JOY
text: This is not working. Are we getting close?

speaker: SADNESS
text: Yeah. Just another right. And a left. Then another left, and a right...

speaker: JOY
text: Are you sure you know where we’re going? Cause we seem to be walking AWAY from Headquarters..
parenthetical: (The SKY BRIGHTENS. Day. And they’re still lost.)

speaker: JOY
text: Riley’s awake.
parenthetical: (Joy drops a core memory. Sadness reaches to grab it.) | [CONT’D]

speaker: JOY
text: Ah ah ah, don’t touch, remember? If you touch them, they stay sad!
parenthetical: [CONT’D]

speaker: SADNESS
text: Oh. Sorry. I won’t...
parenthetical: (Joy looks behind her. The bottom row of memories are BLUE.)

speaker: SADNESS
text: ...starting now.
parenthetical: [CONT’D]

speaker: JOY
text: I can’t take much more of this.
parenthetical: [defeated]

speaker: MALE FORGETTER
text: Forget ‘em!

speaker: JOY
text: Mind Workers!
parenthetical: (Joy runs off toward the voice.)

speaker: SADNESS
text: But Joy, we’re almost... ohhhh. Advertisement
parenthetical: (LATER Joy finds two workers vacuuming up memories from the shelves.)

speaker: FEMALE FORGETTER
text: Phone numbers. We don’t need all of these. They’re in her phone.

speaker: MALE FORGETTER
text: Just forget all of that. Please.

speaker: JOY
text: Excuse me. Hi.

speaker: Male Forgetter
text: Forget it!

speaker: Joy
text: I need to find Friendship Island...

speaker: FEMALE FORGETTER
text: Look at this. Four years of piano lessons.

speaker: MALE FORGETTER
text: Yeah, looks pretty faded.

speaker: FEMALE FORGETTER
text: You know what? Save "Chopsticks” and “Heart and Soul,” get rid of the rest.
parenthetical: (Zoop! The memories get vacuumed up.)

speaker: JOY
text: Are you--

speaker: FEMALE FORGETTER
text: U.S. Presidents. What do you think?

speaker: MALE FORGETTER
text: Eh, just keep Washington, Lincoln and the fat one.

speaker: FEMALE FORGETTER
text: Forget ‘em!

speaker: JOY
text: Hey! You can’t throw those away! Those are perfectly good memories.

speaker: MALE FORGETTER
text: The names of every “Cutie Pie Princess” doll?

speaker: JOY
text: Yes! That is critical information! Glitterstorm, Honeypants, Officer Justice...

speaker: FEMALE FORGETTER
text: Forget ‘em!
parenthetical: (He vacuums up the memories behind Joy. The memories shoot out a sewer pipe and fall into the darkness of the Memory Dump.)

speaker: JOY
text: Hey! Bring those back.

speaker: FEMALE FORGETTER
text: They’re in the dump. Nothing comes back from the dump.

speaker: MALE FORGETTER
text: Yeah. Look lady, this is our job, OK?

speaker: FEMALE FORGETTER
text: When Riley doesn’t care about a memory, it fades.

speaker: JOY
text: Fades?

speaker: FEMALE FORGETTER
text: Happens to the best of ‘em.

speaker: MALE FORGETTER
text: Yeah. Except for this bad boy! This one will NEVER fade.

speaker: JOY
text: The song from the gum commercial?

speaker: MALE FORGETTER
text: Y'know, sometimes we send that one up to headquarters for no reason.

speaker: FEMALE FORGETTER
text: It just plays in Riley’s head over and over again, like a million times. Let’s watch it again. She rewinds it and plays it again. They sing along.

speaker: MALE FORGETTER
text: Tripledent gum will make you smile! Tripledent gum! It lasts a while! Tripledent gum will help you, mister, to punch bad breath right in the kisser.

speaker: JOY
text: Uh, I know. We all know the song. Okay. Yup. Real catchy.

speaker: MALE FORGETTER
text: Hey, hey! What do ya think? Should we do it?

speaker: FEMALE FORGETTER
text: Yeah! Ha ha!

speaker: MALE FORGETTER
text: OK, here we go! Ha ha ha!
parenthetical: (He tips the gum memory into the inner workings of the shelf. FWOOM! It shoots up towards Headquarters.) | (Fear, Anger and Disgust are having a peaceful morning. The memory drops into Headquarters, playing at full volume.)

speaker: MEMORY
text: Tripledent gum will make you smile! Tripledent gum! It lasts a while--

speaker: ANGER
text: What the-- This again?!
parenthetical: (The Forgetters walk away, laughing and singing.)

speaker: JOY
text: Ha. Funny. Wait! Do you know how to get to Friendship Island?! Advertisement

speaker: Riley
text: TripleDent gum...

speaker: Meg
text: Do you like it there? Did you feel any earthquakes? Is the bridge cool?

speaker: Riley
text: Yeah, it's good. What happened with the playoffs?

speaker: Meg
text: We won the first game. Coach says we might actually go to the finals this year. Oh, and we got this new girl on the team. She's so cool!

speaker: Disgust
text: Ah, she did not just say that!

speaker: Fear
text: A new girl? Meg has a new friend already?

speaker: Anger
text: Grr!

speaker: Disgust
text: Hey-hey! Stay happy. We do not wanna lose any more islands here, guys.

speaker: Meg
text: We can pass the puck to each other without even looking. It's like mind reading.

speaker: Anger
text: You like to read minds, Meg? I got something for you to read right here!

speaker: Fear
text: Wait! Wa-wa-wa-wait. let's just be calm for 1 sec...

speaker: Anger
text: Aaargh!

speaker: Riley
text: I gotta go.

speaker: Meg
text: What?

speaker: Riley
text: I GOTTA GO!
parenthetical: (Joy follows the Forgetters, trying to get directions. They ignore her, singing the gum-commercial jingle.)

speaker: Joy
text: Wait, ok. Could you just please tell me which way is...
parenthetical: (A horrible mechanical GROANING sound. Joy reacts.)

speaker: Joy
text: Oh, no!
parenthetical: (Joy rounds a corner and sees Friendship Island crumbling.)

speaker: Joy
text: Friendship Island! What?
parenthetical: (She looks on in horror as Friendship Island falls.)

speaker: JOY
text: Ohh, not Friendship. SADNESS Oh, Riley loved that one. And now it's GONE. Goodbye friendship, hello loneliness.
parenthetical: (Joy hugs the core memories. One of them is memory of young Riley and Meg walking together.) | (Joy sees Hockey Island in the distance.) | [CONT’D]

speaker: JOY
text: We’ll just have to go the long way.
parenthetical: (They turn to look back into the deep memory shelves.)

speaker: SADNESS
text: Yeah. The long, long, long, long way. I’m ready.
parenthetical: (Sadness lies down on the ground, offering her leg. Joy sighs. She doesn’t want to do this again.)

speaker: JOY
text: There’s gotta be a better way.

speaker: JOY
text: Huh?
parenthetical: (Joy sees a strange figure up ahead, gathering memories.)

speaker: BING BONG
text: I will take you, but not you. Oh, who am I kidding, I can’t leave you!

speaker: JOY
text: Hello!

speaker: JOY
text: Wa- Hey! Wait!

speaker: Joy
text: Wh-wait! Stop!
parenthetical: (Joy chases after him.) | (Joy rounds a corner to find the figure crouched on the ground, hiding his face in his hands like a three year old.)

speaker: JOY
text: Excuse me...?

speaker: BING BONG
text: Ahhhh! Uh, I was-I was looking for, um-- --DIVERSION!

speaker: Joy
text: What?
parenthetical: (He THROWS IT and makes a run for it.)

speaker: BING BONG
text: Ha ha! So long, sucker!
parenthetical: (He immediately crashes into a cart of memories.)

speaker: BING BONG
text: Ow! Oww, I hurt all over.

speaker: JOY
text: Wait. I know you.

speaker: BING BONG
text: No, you don’t. I get that a lot, I look like a lot of people.

speaker: JOY
text: No, no, no. I do! Bing Bong! Riley’s Imaginary Friend!

speaker: BING BONG
text: You really DO know me?

speaker: JOY
text: Well of course! Riley loved playing with you, you two were best friends! Oh! Oh, you would know. We’re trying to get back to Headquarters...

speaker: BING BONG
text: Headquarters? You guys are from Headquarters?

speaker: JOY
text: Well, yeah. I’m Joy. This is Sadness.

speaker: BING BONG
text: You’re Joy? THE Joy?

speaker: JOY
text: Mm-hmm.

speaker: BING BONG
text: Well what the heck you doing out here?

speaker: JOY
text: That's a good question! You want to answer that, Sadness?

speaker: BING BONG
text: Oh. Without you, Riley won’t ever be happy. We can't have that, we gotta get you back! I’ll tell you what, follow me.

speaker: JOY
text: Oh, thank you!
parenthetical: (Bing Bong leads them on through the memory shelves.)

speaker: JOY
text: It is so great to see you again. I gotta tell you, I am such a huge fan of your work. Do you remember when you and Riley were in a band?
parenthetical: (FLASH CUTS of their adventures: *Riley bangs on pots and pans, Bing Bong plays his trunk.)

speaker: JOY
text: I went to all of your concerts.

speaker: BING BONG
text: Yeah, I blow a mean nose. *Bing Bong runs on ceiling, Riley chases after him.

speaker: JOY
text: Watching you play tag was such a treat.

speaker: BING BONG
text: Two-time world champ. *Riley and Bing Bong sit in her wagon .

speaker: JOY
text: Oh, and remember your rocket?

speaker: BING BONG
text: Of course! It runs on song power.

speaker: JOY
text: That’s right, your theme song! Who's your friend who likes to play?

speaker: BING BONG
text: BING BONG BING BONG!

speaker: JOY
text: His rocket makes you yell “Hooray!”

speaker: BING BONG/JOY
text: BING BONG BING BONG!
parenthetical: (Sadness eyes Bing Bong, who is a pink elephant made with a cat tail.)

speaker: SADNESS
text: What exactly are you supposed to be?

speaker: BING BONG
text: You know, it’s unclear. I’m mostly cotton candy, but shape-wise, I’m part cat, part elephant, part dolphin.

speaker: JOY
text: Dolphin?
parenthetical: (Bing Bong does a spot-on dolphin impression.)

speaker: BING BONG
text: You gotta remember, when Riley was three, animals were all the rage. The cow goes moo. The horse goes neigh. That’s all people talked about back then.

speaker: JOY
text: Yeah, yeah. I guess that’s true. What are you doing out here?

speaker: BING BONG
text: Well, there’s not much call for imaginary friends lately, so, uh, you know, I’m...

speaker: JOY
text: Hey, hey, don’t be sad. Tell you what, when I get back up to Headquarters, I’ll make sure Riley remembers you.

speaker: BING BONG
text: You will?

speaker: JOY
text: Of course, she’d love that!

speaker: BING BONG
text: Ha ha! This is the greatest day of my life!
parenthetical: (He launches into a jig, but hurts himself and bursts into tears. Candy pours out of his tear ducts.)

speaker: JOY
text: Oh, are you okay?

speaker: SADNESS
text: What’s going on?

speaker: BING BONG
text: I cry candy. Try the caramel, it’s delicious.

speaker: JOY
text: Ooo. Woah!

speaker: BING BONG
text: Oooh. Here, use this.

speaker: JOY
text: Thanks!

speaker: BING BONG
text: Oh, hold on. Wait a second. That always gets stuck at the bottom.
parenthetical: (He empties out an impossible amount of stuff: tons of memories, a boot, an anchor, a cat... Joy and Sadness stare in amazement.)

speaker: BING BONG
text: Here. What? It’s imaginary.
parenthetical: (Joy accepts the satchel and puts in the core memories.) | [CONT’D]

speaker: JOY
text: Thanks! This’ll make it a lot easier to walk back to Headquarters.

speaker: BING BONG
text: Walk? We’re not walkin’! We’re taking the Train of Thought!
parenthetical: (He points to the train speeding towards Headquarters.)

speaker: JOY
text: The train, of course! That is so much faster! But how do we catch it?

speaker: BING BONG
text: Well, it kind of goes all over the place, but there is a station in Imaginationland. I know a short cut. Come on, this way!

speaker: JOY
text: Oh, I’m so glad we ran into you!
parenthetical: (A giant BUILDING blocks their progress. Bing Bong opens a door. It’s dark inside.)

speaker: BING BONG
text: The station is right through here.
parenthetical: (The train station is visible through a door on the other side.)

speaker: BING BONG
text: After you.
parenthetical: [CONT’D]

speaker: SADNESS
text: Joy...

speaker: JOY
text: What?

speaker: SADNESS
text: I read about this place in the manual. We shouldn’t go in there.

speaker: JOY
text: Bing Bong says it’s the quickest way to Headquarters.

speaker: SADNESS
text: No, but Joy, this is Abstract Thought.

speaker: BING BONG
text: What’re you talking about? I go in here all the time. It’s a shortcut, ya see?
parenthetical: (He points to a “Danger” sign above the door by spelling it instead of pronouncing it.)

speaker: BING BONG
text: D-A-N-G-E-R: shortcut. I’ll prove it to ya. Look at me! I’m closer to the station, ‘cause I’m taking the shortcut!
parenthetical: [CONT’D]

speaker: SADNESS
text: Le-let’s go around. This way.

speaker: BING BONG
text: Almost there...!
parenthetical: (Joy looks back at Bing Bong. The station is right there.)

speaker: JOY
text: If you want to walk the long way, go for it. But Riley needs to be happy. I‘m not missing that train. Bing Bong knows what he’s doing. He's part dolphin. They're very smart.

speaker: SADNESS
text: Well, I guess... Advertisement
parenthetical: (She climbs in.)

speaker: Female Mind Worker
text: OK, what abstract concept are we trying to comprehend today?

speaker: Male Mind Worker
text: Um, loneliness.

speaker: Female Mind Worker
text: Hm, looks like there's something in there. I'm gonna turn it on for a minute and burn out the gunk.

speaker: Bing Bong
text: What'd I tell ya? You'll be at Headquarters in no time! Hey, would you look at that?

speaker: Joy
text: Woah! What's happening?

speaker: Sadness
text: Oh, no, they turned it on.

speaker: Bing Bong
text: Huh? Never seen this before.

speaker: Both
text: Aahh! AAAAHHH!!!

speaker: Bing Bong
text: My face! My beautiful face!

speaker: Joy
text: What is going on?

speaker: Sadness
text: We're abstracting! There are four stages. This is the first: Non-objective fragmentation!

speaker: Bing Bong
text: Alright, do not panic! What is important is that we all stay together. Oh!

speaker: Joy
text: Ah!
parenthetical: [Joy's head falls off followed by Sadness who topples.]

speaker: Sadness
text: We're in the second stage, we’re deconstructing!

speaker: Bing Bong
text: RUN! Ah! I can't feel my legs! Oh, there they are.
parenthetical: [falls into pieces]

speaker: Joy
text: Come here, me!
parenthetical: (They gather their pieces as they rush to the exit.)

speaker: Sadness
text: We gotta get outta here! OW! Before we're nothing but shape and color! OW! We'll get stuck here forever!

speaker: Joy
text: Stuck?! Why did we come in here?

speaker: Bing Bong
text: I told you, it's a shortcut!

speaker: Joy
text: The Train!
parenthetical: (The view squeezes out to black; when it squeezes back in, now everything has become 2D-flash animated with Joy, Sadness, and Bing Bong as flat shapes.)

speaker: Sadness
text: Oh, no, we're two dimensional! That's stage three!

speaker: Joy
text: We're getting nowhere!

speaker: Bing Bong
text: Depth! I'm lacking depth!

speaker: Joy
text: Come on! We can't fit!
parenthetical: (He suddenly changes into a single spiral representation; Joy turns into a starburst. Bing Bong into a cone and Sadness turns into a raindrop, as everything but the door vanishes.)

speaker: Sadness
text: Oh, no, we're non-figurative! This is the last stage!

speaker: Bing Bong
text: We're not gonna make it!
parenthetical: (She flops on the ground; suddenly, the side of her shape is revealed to be just a line.)

speaker: Sadness
text: Wait, we're two dimensional. Fall on your face!
parenthetical: (The three lines land outside just as the train pulls out. They get up to go, but as lines they can barely move.)

speaker: Joy
text: Stop! STOP!
parenthetical: (Joy goes from non-representational to two-dimensional. She falls down flat as the train speeds off. POP! The three transform back to normal.)

speaker: JOY
text: No! You said that was a shortcut.
parenthetical: [CONT’D]

speaker: BING BONG
text: I did, but wow, we should NOT have gone in there. That was dangerous! They really should put up a sign.

speaker: JOY
text: How long till the next train? Advertisement

speaker: BING BONG
text: Who knows? But don’t worry. There’s another station. That way! The train always stops there, right before it goes to Headquarters. If we hurry, we can catch it!

speaker: JOY
text: This isn’t another one of your shortcuts, is it?

speaker: BING BONG
text: Yeah!
parenthetical: (Joy skeptically watches him head off.)

speaker: JOY
text: Is there really another station?

speaker: SADNESS
text: Uh-huh. Through there.
parenthetical: (Bing Bong leads them towards the impressive gates of...)

speaker: BING BONG
text: Welcome to Imagination Land!

speaker: JOY
text: Imagination Land?

speaker: BING BONG
text: Sure! I come here all the time. I’m practically the mayor. Hey, you guys hungry? There’s French Fry Forest! Nom nom nom, delicious!
parenthetical: (Joy is loving this.)

speaker: JOY
text: No way!

speaker: BING BONG
text: Oooh, oooh! Check it out! Trophy Town! Medals! Ribbons! Everyone’s a winner!
parenthetical: (Bing Bong kicks a soccer ball into a giant goal and workers appear out of nowhere to shower him with awards.)

speaker: BING BONG
text: I won first place!
parenthetical: (Workers give Joy a medal and Sadness a ribbon.)

speaker: JOY
text: Me too!

speaker: SADNESS
text: Ohh... Participation award...

speaker: JOY
text: Wait, is that-- Sadness, look, it’s Cloud Town! That is my favorite!
parenthetical: (Joy rips off a chunk of cloud and floats up into the air.)

speaker: JOY
text: It's so soft!
parenthetical: (Joy jumps down and runs off.) | [CONT’D]

speaker: BING BONG
text: Let me try!
parenthetical: (Bing Bong rips off some cloud. A CLOUD MAN emerges from the house, angry.)

speaker: CLOUD MAN
text: Hey! What’s the big idea? You’d better fix that wall, or else you’re in big troub--
parenthetical: (POOF! Bing Bong blows the Cloud Man away and walks off nervously.) | (Joy and Bing Bong gleefully jump between couch cushions.)

speaker: JOY
text: Oh, no, lava! Whooo!

speaker: BING BONG
text: Whahoooo! Imagination Land is the best!
parenthetical: (Sadness tries to jump to a rock, barely makes it.)

speaker: SADNESS
text: Is it all going to be so interactive?

speaker: BING BONG
text: Hey, look! The House of Cards!! Ooh, wait, hang on just a minute...
parenthetical: (From a nearby garage made of cards, Bing Bong pulls a wagon.)

speaker: JOY
text: Your rocket!

speaker: BING BONG
text: Yeah! I stashed it in there for safekeeping. And now I’m all set to take Riley to the MOON!

speaker: BING BONG
text: Oh, I’m sorry.
parenthetical: [CONT’D]

speaker: HOUSE OF CARDS WORKER
text: Great.

speaker: JOY
text: Oh. I love Imagination land!

speaker: BING BONG
text: Isn’t it great? And there’s always something new, like...
parenthetical: (A giant machine. A conveyor belt reveals a handsome teenager.)

speaker: BING BONG
text: Who the heck is that?
parenthetical: [CONT’D]

speaker: BOYFRIEND GENERATOR WORKER
text: Imaginary Boyfriend.

speaker: IMAGINARY BOYFRIEND
text: I would die for Riley.
parenthetical: (Joy grimaces.)

speaker: BING BONG
text: I’ve never seen him before.

speaker: IMAGINARY BOYFRIEND
text: I live in Canada.

speaker: BING BONG
text: Anyway. This way, through Preschool World! We’re nearly to the train!

speaker: JOY
text: Riley, here we come!

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: This should be fun. New team, new friends... These kids look pretty good. Considering they're from San Francisco. Heheh!

speaker: Riley
text: Gotta go.

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: OK. Good luck, sweetie!

speaker: Disgust
text: Luck isn't going to help us now! If she tries to use Hockey Island, it's going down!

speaker: Fear
text: Which is why, I've recalled every hockey memory I can think of. One of these has gotta work in place of the core memory.

speaker: Anger
text: She's about to play.

speaker: Disgust
text: Hurry.

speaker: Fear
text: Ha ha! We did it gang. It's working!

speaker: Anger
text: Oh!

speaker: Hockey Coach
text: Line change! Line change! Change it up! Change it up!

speaker: Fear
text: Haha! No-no-no-no! Aahh!

speaker: Disgust
text: It's like we don't learn anything.

speaker: Hockey Coach
text: Let's pick it up out there.

speaker: Anger
text: That's it!

speaker: Fear
text: No no, no. breathe... find your happy place.

speaker: Anger
text: Aaarggh!

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: Riley, what's wrong?

speaker: Riley
text: Let's go.

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: You're not going to finish tryouts?

speaker: Riley
text: What's the point?

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: Hey, it'll be alright. Let's just go back...

speaker: Riley
text: Stop saying everything will be alright!!!

speaker: Hockey Coach
text: Nice hustling, ladies! Advertisement

speaker: JOY
text: Hockey? Oh, no, no. She loves hockey. She can’t give up hockey. Bing Bong, we have to get to that station.

speaker: BING BONG
text: Sure thing. This way, just past Graham Cracker Castle. Hey. That’s weird. Graham Cracker Castle used to be right here. I wonder why they moved it?
parenthetical: (He walks on, concerned, LEAVING HIS WAGON BEHIND HIM.)

speaker: BING BONG
text: Wow, that’s not... I would have sworn Sparkle Pony Mountain was right here. Hey, what’s going on?
parenthetical: [CONT’D]

speaker: JOY
text: Yeah, yeah, I, I dunno, we’ll have to come back--

speaker: BING BONG
text: Princess Dream World!
parenthetical: (A wrecking ball hits a pink castle. Glitter dust plumes.)

speaker: BING BONG
text: Oh, no! The Stuffed Animal Hall of Fame!
parenthetical: (RIIIP! The head of a bear comes off. Too late, Bing Bong notices some construction workers CARRYING AWAY HIS ROCKET.) | [CONT’D]

speaker: BING BONG
text: My rocket! Wait! Riley and I, we’re still using that rocket! It still has some song power left!! Who's your friend who likes to play?
parenthetical: (THE ROCKET RESPONDS weakly, propelling it forward. The workers toss it onto a junk pile. A bulldozer pushes the pile towards the CLIFF EDGE.) | [CONT’D]

speaker: BING BONG
text: Nooo!!! No! No! No! You can’t take my rocket to the dump! Riley and I are going to the moon! Ahhh!
parenthetical: (Over it goes, into the dump.) | [CONT’D]

speaker: BING BONG
text: Riley can’t be done with me.
parenthetical: (Bing Bong sits, stunned. Joy approaches gingerly.) | [CONT’D]

speaker: JOY
text: Hey, it’s going to be okay. We can fix this! We just need to get back to Headquarters. Which way to the train station?

speaker: BING BONG
text: I had a whole trip planned for us.
parenthetical: (Joy tries another tactic.) | [still stunned]

speaker: JOY
text: Hey, who’s ticklish, huh? Here comes the tickle monster!
parenthetical: (No response.)

speaker: JOY
text: Hey! Bing Bong, look at this! Dohoioih!
parenthetical: (She makes a silly face. Nothing.) | [CONT’D]

speaker: JOY
text: Oh, here’s a fun game! You point to the train station and we all go there! Won’t that be fun? Come on, let’s go to the train station!
parenthetical: (Joy walks off, attempting to lead.) | [CONT’D]

speaker: SADNESS
text: I’m sorry they took your rocket. They took something that you loved. It’s gone, forever.
parenthetical: [sits beside Bing Bong]

speaker: JOY
text: Sadness, don’t make him feel worse.

speaker: SADNESS
text: Sorry.

speaker: BING BONG
text: It’s all I had left of Riley.

speaker: SADNESS
text: I bet you and Riley had great adventures.

speaker: BING BONG
text: Oh, they were wonderful. Once we flew back in time. We had breakfast twice that day.

speaker: Joy
text: Sadness!

speaker: SADNESS
text: That’s sounds amazing. I bet Riley liked it.

speaker: BING BONG
text: Oh, she did. We were best friends.

speaker: SADNESS
text: Yeah. that’s sad.
parenthetical: (Bing Bong puts his head on Sadness’ shoulder and CRIES. Sadness keeps her arm around him until he’s done.)

speaker: BING BONG
text: I’m okay now. C’mon, the train station is this way.
parenthetical: (He walks off. Joy and Sadness follow.)

speaker: JOY
text: How did you do that?

speaker: SADNESS
text: I don’t know. He was sad, so I listened to what--

speaker: BING BONG
text: Hey, there’s the train!
parenthetical: (Our trio climb aboard just as the train moves.)

speaker: JOY
text: We made it! We’re finally gonna get home!
parenthetical: (Twirling, she knocks over two boxes labeled “FACTS” and “OPINIONS”. Small objects spill out of both.)

speaker: JOY
text: Oh, no! These Facts and Opinions look so similar!
parenthetical: [CONT’D]

speaker: BING BONG
text: Ah, don’t worry about it. Happens all the time. Advertisement
parenthetical: (He dumps them into the “FACTS” box.)

speaker: DISGUST
text: On a scale of one to ten, I give this day an F.

speaker: ANGER
text: Well why don’t we quit standing around and DO something?

speaker: DISGUST
text: Like what, genius?
parenthetical: (Fear enters with a suitcase.)

speaker: FEAR
text: Like quitting! That’s what I’m doing. FEAR Sure, it’s the coward’s way out. But this coward's gonna survive!
parenthetical: (He stomps on the recall button.) | (The tube drops over him, but pummels Fear with dozens of memories. The tube shuts off and retracts. Fear drops to the floor in pain.)

speaker: DISGUST
text: Emotions can’t quit, genius.
parenthetical: (Fear spits up a memory.)

speaker: DISGUST
text: Euch! I thought we were supposed to be keeping Riley happy.
parenthetical: [CONT’D]

speaker: ANGER
text: Wait a minute. Wait a minute!
parenthetical: (Anger runs to the back and rummages the cubby through the ideas.)

speaker: ANGER
text: Ah ha!
parenthetical: [CONT’D]

speaker: FEAR
text: What is it?

speaker: ANGER
text: Oh, nothing. Just the best idea ever.

speaker: DISGUST
text: What?

speaker: ANGER
text: All the good core memories were made in Minnesota. Ergo, we go back to Minnesota and make more. Ta-da!

speaker: FEAR
text: Wa-Wa-Wait, you’re saying we run away?

speaker: ANGER
text: Well, I wouldn’t call it that. I’d call it, “The Happy Core Memory Development Program.”

speaker: FEAR
text: You can’t be serious!

speaker: ANGER
text: Hey. Our life was perfect, until Mom and Dad decided to move to San Fran Stinktown.

speaker: FEAR
text: But, I mean, it’s just so drastic!

speaker: ANGER
text: Need I remind you of how great things were there? Our room? Our backyard? Our friends?
parenthetical: (He punches up a memory. It plays on the screen.)

speaker: MEMORY
text: Tripledent gum will make you smile! Tripledent gum it lasts--

speaker: ANGER
text: Did I ask for the gum commercial?! Anyway, it was better, that’s my point.

speaker: DISGUST
text: Yeah. Riley was happier in Minnesota...

speaker: FEAR
text: Wait, hold on. Shouldn’t we just sleep on this or something?

speaker: ANGER
text: Fine. Let’s sleep on it. Because hey, I’m sure jolly fun-filled times are just around the corner. Advertisement

speaker: JOY
text: Huh?
parenthetical: (The engineer and his assistant hop off the engine.)

speaker: JOY
text: Hey hey! Why aren’t we moving?
parenthetical: [CONT’D]

speaker: TRAIN ENGINEER
text: Riley’s gone to sleep. We’re all on break.

speaker: SADNESS
text: You mean we’re stuck here until morning?

speaker: BING BONG
text: Yeah, the Train of Thought doesn’t run while she’s asleep.

speaker: JOY
text: Oh, we can’t wait that long!

speaker: SADNESS
text: How about we wake her up?

speaker: JOY
text: Sadness, that’s ridiculous. How could we possibly...
parenthetical: (She follows Sadness’ gaze and spots DREAM PRODUCTIONS.)

speaker: JOY
text: How about we wake her up?
parenthetical: [CONT’D]

speaker: SADNESS
text: Great idea, Joy.

speaker: JOY
text: Thanks. Come on!
parenthetical: (The trio enter Dream Productions.)

speaker: JOY
text: Whoa! This place is huge.

speaker: SADNESS
text: Yeah, it looks so much smaller than I expected.
parenthetical: (They walk past dream “movie posters.”)

speaker: BING BONG
text: Whoa! “I Can Fly”? I love that one!
parenthetical: (Joy spots a Unicorn sitting in a director’s chair.)

speaker: JOY
text: Rainbow Unicorn! She’s RIGHT THERE!
parenthetical: (Joy tries to play it cool as they walk past.)

speaker: SADNESS
text: My friend says you’re famous. She wants your autograph.

speaker: JOY
text: No, no, Sadness, don’t bother Miss Unicorn, okay? Sorry, she’s from outta town. So embarrassing, right?
parenthetical: (She pushes Sadness away. Joy pops back in.)

speaker: JOY
text: I loved you in Fairy Dream Adventure Part 7. Okay, bye. I love you.
parenthetical: (They reach STAGE B. A sign below a red flashing light reads, “DO NOT ENTER WHEN LIGHT FLASHING.”) | [CONT’D]

speaker: BING BONG
text: Huh. Wonder what that means. Oh, well, let’s go in!

speaker: Dream Director
text: Set up the classroom set. Today's memories are in. We've got a lot to work with here. Riley dumped her best friend, had a miserable day at school and quit hockey. The writers have put together a killer script.

speaker: Fear
text: Just because Joy and Sadness are gone, I have to do stupid dream duty.

speaker: Joy
text: Okay, how are we gonna wake her up?

speaker: Sadness
text: Well, she wakes up sometimes when she has a scary dream. We could scare her.

speaker: Joy
text: Scare her? No, no, she’s been through enough already.

speaker: Sadness
text: But Joy--

speaker: Joy
text: Sadness, you may know your way around down here, but I know Riley! We’re gonna make her so happy she’ll wake up with exhilaration! We’ll excite her awake!

speaker: Sadness
text: That’s never happened before.
parenthetical: [Joy spots a nearby dog costume and tosses it to Sadness.]

speaker: Joy
text: Ooh, Riley loves dogs. Put this on!

speaker: Sadness
text: I don’t think that’ll work.

speaker: Joy
text: Bing Bong.

speaker: BING BONG
text: Yeah?
parenthetical: [She gives him the satchel containing the CORE MEMORIES.]

speaker: Joy
text: Don’t let anything happen to these.

speaker: BING BONG
text: Got it!
parenthetical: (DREAM DIRECTOR checks the monitor.)

speaker: DREAM DIRECTOR
text: Mm hm. Add the reality distortion filter.
parenthetical: (A WORKER slips a filter over the camera lens, transforming the MIND WORKERS into replicates of Riley’s classmates.)

speaker: DREAM DIRECTOR
text: Love it. Remember, play to the camera, everyone! Riley is the camera! Makeup, get out of there, we are on in 5, 4, 3...
