parenthetical: (A UNION HARP PLAYER strums: the dream is starting. From the camera’s POV: the classroom set. STUDENTS at desks. An EXTRA playing Riley’s teacher reads cue cards badly.) | [CONT’D]

speaker: Fear
text: Man, she is one bad actress.
parenthetical: (The CUE CARD GUY flips over the next card.)

speaker: DREAM DIRECTOR
text: Camera.

speaker: DREAM DIRECTOR
text: And... cue Riley.
parenthetical: [A CREW MEMBER clears his throat and reads into a megaphone.]

speaker: RILEY VOICE
text: My name is Riley Andersen, I’m from Minnesota and now I live here.

speaker: Fear
text: Teeth falling out? Pfft. Yeah, I'm used to that one. Let me guess, we have no pants on.

speaker: Fear
text: Called it!
parenthetical: [Joy and Sadness are in the dog costume, waiting to go on.]

speaker: Joy
text: Ready?

speaker: Sadness
text: I don’t think this happy thing is going to work. But if we scare her--
parenthetical: [worried]

speaker: Joy
text: Just follow my lead. Here we go!
parenthetical: [Joy yanks Sadness onto the set. They run around in playful circles. Joy started barking.]

speaker: DREAM DIRECTOR
text: Who is that?

speaker: Fear
text: Uh, what’s going on?
parenthetical: [confused] | [Joy and Sadness run around, Joy licking students and barking. Through the reality distortion they look like a happy puppy. Joy looks at the sleep indicator: Riley is still asleep. ]

speaker: Joy
text: Psst. You’re on! Go!
parenthetical: [Bing Bong pulls a rope. Balloons drop! A colorful backdrop!]

speaker: Joy
text: Woo! Let’s party! Let’s dance. Woo!

speaker: Fear
text: Hey, a party.

speaker: Sadness
text: Joy, this isn’t working.
parenthetical: [Bing Bong knocks over a light, causing spooky uplighting. Sadness tries to stop. The dog costume RIPS IN HALF. Fear spits out the tea he was drinking. On screen, a real dog is in two pieces.]

speaker: Joy
text: Huh? Sadness, what are you doing? Come back here!
parenthetical: [She starts chasing Sadness. On screen, the front half of a dog chases the rear half. ]

speaker: Fear
text: It’s just a dream, it’s just a dream, it’s just a dream...

speaker: DREAM DIRECTOR
text: They’re trying to wake her up! Call security!

speaker: Bing Bong
text: Hi, Riley, it’s me!

speaker: Fear
text: Bing Bong?
parenthetical: [dumbstruck]

speaker: Bing Bong
text: Who’s your friend who likes to play? Bing Bong, Bing Bong!

speaker: Joy
text: Sadness, stop!

speaker: Joy
text: Sadness! You are ruining this dream! You’re scaring her! Sadness But look, it’s working!
parenthetical: [The SLEEP INDICATOR is starting to tip. Joy’s eyes widen.]

speaker: Joy
text: Whoa.

speaker: DREAM DIRECTOR
text: They are not part of this dream! Get them!
parenthetical: [Sadness pulls Joy out of harms way as guards GRAB Bing Bong.]

speaker: DREAM PRODUCTIONS COP #1
text: Stop right there!

speaker: BING BONG
text: Ow! Hey!

speaker: DREAM DIRECTOR
text: Pan away! Pan away!
parenthetical: [The camera pans away to Rainbow Unicorn making off with a giant cupcake platter from the craft services table. ]

speaker: Fear
text: Booooo! Pick a plot line!
parenthetical: [Joy and Sadness watch as guards haul off Bing Bong and began to follow.]

speaker: Bing Bong
text: Ow! Careful! ???! You can't do this! I know people in headquarters!

speaker: DREAM PRODUCTIONS COP #2
text: Hold still!
parenthetical: [Joy and Sadness watch Bing Bong being dragged far off to the dark, menacing entry of SUBCONSCIOUS.]

speaker: BING BONG
text: Ow! Ow! Cut that out. Ow! Please... you can’t do this! Do you like candy? You look hungry. I can get you candy! Please! Ow. Ow. Ow.

speaker: Joy
text: No, no, no, no, no, no, no, no, no, no! there go the core memories...!

speaker: BING BONG
text: I can’t go in there. I’m scared of the dark. Please!

speaker: DREAM PRODUCTIONS COP
text: ??? out of you!
parenthetical: [He’s thrown in and the door SLAMS SHUT. Joy and Sadness head after him, down the long stairway.]

speaker: Joy
text: Come on! What is this place?

speaker: SADNESS
text: The Subconscious. It’s where they take all the troublemakers. Advertisement

speaker: SUBCONSCIOUS GUARD DAVE
text: My hat feels loose.

speaker: Joy
text: How do we get in?

speaker: SUBCONSCIOUS GUARD FRANK
text: Let me see.
parenthetical: (Sadness has an idea. She leads Joy around and behind the guards.)

speaker: SUBCONSCIOUS GUARD DAVE
text: I, I mean. You got my hat? Or, or is that your hat?

speaker: SUBCONSCIOUS GUARD FRANK
text: Yeah, it’s my hat.

speaker: SUBCONSCIOUS GUARD DAVE
text: You sure? I don’t know, look in the label.

speaker: SUBCONSCIOUS GUARD FRANK
text: Yeah, it says, “My Hat.”

speaker: SUBCONSCIOUS GUARD DAVE
text: Wait, it says, “My Hat”?

speaker: SUBCONSCIOUS GUARD FRANK
text: “My Hat,” it says.

speaker: SUBCONSCIOUS GUARD DAVE
text: That’s what I wrote in my hat.

speaker: SUBCONSCIOUS GUARD FRANK
text: What are you talking about?

speaker: SUBCONSCIOUS GUARD DAVE
text: You’ve got my hat on.

speaker: SUBCONSCIOUS GUARD FRANK
text: Okay, but it’s my handwriting--
parenthetical: (Sadness loudly SHAKES the door.)

speaker: SUBCONSCIOUS GUARD DAVE
text: Hey! You!

speaker: SADNESS
text: Oh! You caught us! Heh.

speaker: SUBCONSCIOUS GUARD FRANK
text: Get back in there! No escaping!
parenthetical: (The guards shove them through and slam the door. Joy gives Sadness an approving nod: nice work. They head forward. This place is spooky.)

speaker: SADNESS
text: I don’t like it here. It’s where they keep Riley’s darkest fears.

speaker: Joy
text: It’s broccoli!

speaker: Sadness
text: The stairs to the basement!

speaker: Joy
text: Grandma’s vacuum cleaner!
parenthetical: [The vacuum gives chase. Joy and Sadness lose it and hide behind a rock.]

speaker: Joy
text: Okay, come on.
parenthetical: [Sadness makes a loud CRINKLING NOISE with each step.]

speaker: Joy
text: Would you walk quieter?

speaker: Sadness
text: I’m trying! Joy What is going-- Candy wrappers.
parenthetical: (They follow a trail of candy wrappers into the cave to find a crying Bing Bong in a balloon cage, atop a large mound.)

speaker: Joy
text: Bing Bong!

speaker: BING BONG
text: Joy?

speaker: Joy
text: There you are.
parenthetical: [The imaginary friend shushes and points. They are standing in front of a giant SLEEPING CLOWN,]

speaker: Joy
text: It’s Jangles.

speaker: Jangles
text: Who's the birthday girl, who's the birthday girl?
parenthetical: [They gingerly approach Jangles. Joy climbs up to Bing Bong.]

speaker: Joy
text: Do you have the core memories?

speaker: Bing Bong
text: Yeah.
parenthetical: (He gives her the satchel. Joy holds them close.)

speaker: Bing Bong
text: All he cared about was the candy.
parenthetical: [Joy helps Bing Bong squeeze through, but the balloon-bars make loud twisting noises. Jangles stirs. Joy freezes. She stretches them further... Success! Joy and Bing Bong hurry down and the trio rush away.]

speaker: BING BONG
text: We’re out of here! Let’s get to that train.
parenthetical: [CONT’D]

speaker: Joy
text: Wait. The train’s not running. We still have to wake up Riley.

speaker: Sadness
text: But how?

speaker: BING BONG
text: Oh, no.
parenthetical: [Together, Joy and Sadness approach Jangles and HONK HIS NOSE. His eyes open. He stands. He’s HUGE, towering over them.]

speaker: Joy
text: H-hey, Sadness, did you hear about the p-pahh-party that we’re having?

speaker: Sadness
text: Yeah! Ohhhh, yes Joy! Isn’t it a babahh... birthday party?

speaker: JANGLES
text: Did you say... birthday?

speaker: Joy
text: And there’s going to be cake, and presents--

speaker: SADNESS
text: --an-and games and balloons--

speaker: JANGLES
text: A BIRTHDAY?!

speaker: Joy
text: Okay. Follow us!
parenthetical: (All three run towards the gate, followed by the giant clown!)

speaker: Joy
text: Nothing like a good scare to wake you up, right?
parenthetical: (The guards inspect each others’ hats.)

speaker: SUBCONSCIOUS GUARD FRANK
text: Okay, but it’s my handwriting.

speaker: SUBCONSCIOUS GUARD DAVE
text: No, it isn’t. That’s my hand-- I wrote that!

speaker: SUBCONSCIOUS GUARD FRANK
text: No, but THIS one’s my handwriting--
parenthetical: (BASH! Jangles’ mallet smashes through, laughing maniacally. Our trio run up the stairs, Jangles close behind. The UNICORN DREAM is mid-production: a Glitter Dance Party, rainbows and sparkles aplenty. The crew is bored, even Fear in HQ. he set begins to shake. BLAAAM! Jangles crashes in.)

speaker: JANGLES
text: WHO’S THE BIRTHDAY GIRL?!
parenthetical: [Fear SHRIEKS, bashes a button and passes out Riley bolts upright in bed. The meter reads “AWAKE”. Joy and Sadness do a happy dance.]

speaker: BING BONG
text: Come on, let’s go!
parenthetical: [They bolt, leaving Jangles gleefully demolishing the sets. Our trio leap aboard just as the train picks up speed.]

speaker: Joy
text: Ha ha! We made it!

speaker: SADNESS
text: Whoo-hoo! Ha ha!

speaker: Joy
text: Guess who’s on their way to Headquarters?!
parenthetical: (Joy grabs Sadness and picks her up in celebration.)

speaker: SADNESS
text: We are! Advertisement

speaker: Disgust
text: What is going on?

speaker: Anger
text: He did it again.

speaker: Fear
text: We were at school and we were naked, and there was a dog... But the back half was... was chasing it... It was Bing Bong.

speaker: Anger
text: You idiot! It was a dream! This is ridiculous. We can't even get a good night sleep anymore!
parenthetical: (He takes out the idea bulb from before.)

speaker: Anger
text: Time to take action. Stupid Mom and Dad, if they hadn't moved us, none of this would have happened. Who's with me?

speaker: Fear
text: Oh, uh... no, no, no, no, no, no.

speaker: Disgust
text: Yeah, let's do it.
parenthetical: (Anger inserts the bulb into the console; it screws in and lights up with a chime. Outside, Riley gets the idea and takes out her laptop. The bulb then sinks into the console.)

speaker: Anger
text: She took it. There's no turning back.

speaker: Disgust
text: So, how are we going to get to Minnesota from here?

speaker: Anger
text: Well, why don't we go down to the elephant lot and rent an elephant?

speaker: Fear
text: Hey, that sounds nice!

speaker: Anger
text: We're taking the bus, nitwit!
parenthetical: (Riley pulls up the Transway Bus Lines website, setting up a one-way travel from San Francisco to Minneapolis, departing the very next day.)

speaker: Anger
text: There's a bus leaving tomorrow. Perfect!

speaker: Disgust
text: A ticket costs money. How do we get money?

speaker: Anger
text: Mom's purse.

speaker: Disgust
text: You wouldn't!

speaker: Anger
text: Oh, but I would. Where was it we saw it last? Tripledent gum...

speaker: Anger
text: NO!!

speaker: Fear
text: Aah!

speaker: Anger
text: It's downstairs somewhere. Mom and Dad got us into this mess, they can pay to get us out. Advertisement

speaker: Bing Bong
text: How about this, huh? Isn’t this nice? You can see everything from up here! Look, there's Inductive Reasoning. There's Deja Vu. There's Language Processing! There's Deja Vu. There's Critical Thinking! There's Deja Vu. Hey, look at this, guys! Memories!
parenthetical: [Bing Bong ducks inside the train. Joy looks over to Sadness.]

speaker: Joy
text: Hey, that was a good idea. About scaring Riley awake. You’re not so bad.

speaker: Sadness
text: Really?

speaker: Joy
text: Nice work.
parenthetical: (Joy turns to sit on a crate inside the train.)

speaker: Joy
text: Oh, I can’t wait to get the old Riley back. As soon as we get there I’m going to fix this whole mess.
parenthetical: [Bing Bong looks at a memory. Inside there was Riley hoisted in the air by her teammates.]

speaker: Bing Bong
text: Whoa. Is this Riley? She’s so big now. She won’t fit in my rocket. How’re we gonna get to the moon?

speaker: Joy
text: Oh, it's that time in the twisty tree, remember? The hockey team showed up and Mom and Dad were there cheering. Look at her, having fun and laughing. I love this one.

speaker: Sadness
text: I love that one too.

speaker: Joy
text: Atta girl! Now you’re getting it!

speaker: Sadness
text: Yeah. It was the day the Prairie Dogs lost the big playoff game. Riley missed the winning shot. She felt awful. She wanted to quit.

speaker: Sadness
text: Sorry. I went sad again, didn’t I?

speaker: Joy
text: I’ll tell ya what. We can keep working on that when we get back. Okay?

speaker: Sadness
text: Okay.

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: I just don't understand.
parenthetical: (From atop the staircase, Riley watches; Mrs. Andersen walks out of view, leaving her purse on the table.)

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: Why did our moving van even go to Texas?
parenthetical: (Once the coast is clear, Riley makes her move and approaches the purse.)

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: Multiple customers?
parenthetical: (Riley takes the purse and opens it.)

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: Well, we were promised delivery on the 5th.
parenthetical: (Riley gets out the wallet, opens it, and takes out Mrs. Andersen's credit card. Suddenly, Mrs. Andersen turns around.)

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: Uh-huh.
parenthetical: (Riley gets nervous; Mrs. Andersen looks up...and Riley has vanished.)

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: Excuse me, isn't there any way that you can rush it?
parenthetical: (Riley makes it back upstairs, unnoticed and credit card in hand to pay for the bus ticket. Her act of dishonesty causes Honesty Island to crumble and collapse; the Train of Thought passes by allowing Joy to notice.)

speaker: Joy
text: Honesty Island?
parenthetical: [Honesty Island crashes into the train tracks. Joy, Sadness and Bing Bong scream. Workers below see the train falling towards them.]

speaker: BING BONG
text: Hold on!
parenthetical: [Workers grab Joy, Sadness and Bing Bong just as the train slides off the cliff and drops into the dump below. Joy looks towards Headquarters.]

speaker: Joy
text: That was our way home. We lost another island. What is happening?

speaker: Mind Worker
text: Haven't you heard? Riley's running away.

speaker: Joy
text: What? Advertisement

speaker: Fear
text: Wait, wait! Hang on, guys. Are we really doing this? This is serious.

speaker: Anger
text: Look, we have no core memories.
parenthetical: (Anger takes command.)

speaker: Anger
text: You want Riley to be happy? Let's get back to Minnesota and make more.
parenthetical: (With firm resolve, Riley finishes packing and zips her backpack.)

speaker: Sadness
text: Joy, if we hurry, we can still stop her.
parenthetical: (Joy looks toward the last remaining island, soon-to-be gone for good if they don't act up.)

speaker: Joy
text: Family Island. Let's go!
parenthetical: (They all rush towards the island, no time to lose. By now, Riley is coming downstairs; this causes Family Island to shake.)

speaker: Sadness
text: Joy! Joy, it's too dangerous, we won't make it in time.

speaker: Joy
text: But, that's our only way back.
parenthetical: [The rumbling causes an END CAP of a nearby shelf to break, exposing an open tube. A memory gets sucked up and shot towards Headquarters.]

speaker: Joy
text: A recall tube!

speaker: Sadness
text: We can get recalled!

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: Have a great day, sweetheart.

speaker: Mr. Andersen
text: See you after school, monkey.

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: We love you! Family Island rumbles. A LARGE CHUNK of cliff edge breaks off behind them, driving Joy and Sadness forward.
parenthetical: (Riley doesn’t respond. She turns and walks out the door.)

speaker: Joy
text: Go! Run! RUN! Go!
parenthetical: [Joy steps into the disconnected TUBE, ready to sail up to Headquarters. Sadness tries to climb in, but there’s not enough room; she jostles, pressing against the satchel. The CORE MEMORIES start to turn blue.]

speaker: Joy
text: Woah woah! Sadness! Sadness, stop! You’re hurting Riley!
parenthetical: [Joy pulls a core memory from the satchel. It is BRIGHT BLUE, but slowly fades back to normal.]

speaker: Sadness
text: Oh, no, no, no! I did it again!

speaker: Joy
text: If you get in here, these core memories will get sad!
parenthetical: [Joy watches FAMILY ISLAND crumble. She looks down at Sadness. Joy, pained, decides.]

speaker: Joy
text: I’m sorry. Riley needs to be happy.

speaker: Sadness
text: Joy?
parenthetical: (She closes the tube and starts rising up to Headquarters alone, as Sadness and Bing Bong watch. Just then, the shelf below her collapses, breaking the tube, and Joy falls!)

speaker: Bing Bong
text: Joy!
parenthetical: [Bing Bong runs forward to help her, but the ground beneath him crumbles away. Joy and Bing Bong tumble into the abyss.]

speaker: Sadness
text: JOY!

speaker: Joy
text: Do you remember how she used to stick her tongue out when she was coloring?

speaker: Another memory
text: Riley talking to a bug.

speaker: Joy
text: I could listen to her stories all day.

speaker: Another memory
text: Riley spinning.

speaker: Joy
text: I just wanted Riley to be happy. And now... SADNESS It was the day the Prairie Dogs lost the big playoff game. Riley missed the winning shot. She felt awful. She wanted to quit.
parenthetical: (IN THE MEMORY: Mom and Dad come to console Riley. Joy fast forwards. IN THE MEMORY: The hockey team lifts Riley on their shoulders and cheers. Riley smiles.) | [She holds the recent blue memory of Riley in class. Joy hugs them all until the memories fall out of her arms. Joy cries. A long, deep, painful cry. Joy has lost everything. Around her, memories fade and disappear. A tear falls onto the faded TWISTY-TREE MEMORY. Joy wipes it. In wiping the tear, the memory rewinds. Its color CHANGES from GOLD TO BLUE. IN THE MEMORY: Riley sits in the tree with her parents, the team approaching in the distance. Joy looks closer. What’s this? She rewinds more. IN THE MEMORY: Riley sits in the tree, crying, alone. Joy remembers what Sadness said about the memory...]

speaker: Joy
text: Sadness. Mom and Dad, the team... they came to help because of Sadness.
parenthetical: (She turns to Bing Bong, ready to take action.)

speaker: Joy
text: We have to get back up there. BING BONG Joy, we’re stuck down here. We might as well be on another planet.

speaker: Joy
text: Another planet. Who’s your friend who likes to play?
parenthetical: (Silence.)

speaker: Joy
text: Who’s your friend who likes to play?

speaker: BING BONG
text: His rocket makes you yell "Hooray!"
parenthetical: (Far off, BING BONG’S ROCKET answers. They run towards it.)

speaker: BING BONG
text: Who's the best in every way and wants to sing this song to say, who's your friend who likes to play? Bing Bong Bing Bong! His Rocket make you yell "Hooray!"-
parenthetical: (Joy and Bing Bong find the rocket! They perch the wagon atop a hill. Joy looks resigned.) | [CONT’D]

speaker: JOY
text: Hop in!
parenthetical: (Both inside, Bing Bong pushes off. They speed down a slope to gain momentum, singing all the way. They reach the bottom and shoot up another hill, launching up towards the cliff top. Joy and Bing Bong enthusiastically, if not desperately, sing at the top of their lungs. Not even close. They crash back to the bottom.)

speaker: Joy
text: C’mon!

speaker: BING BONG
text: Come on, Joy. One more time. I’ve got a feeling about this one.
parenthetical: (Hurdling down the largest hill yet, they continue to sing Bing Bong’s song.)

speaker: BING BONG
text: Louder! Louder, Joy! Sing louder!
parenthetical: (Bing Bong times it just right... and jumps out of the wagon. Without his weight, the Joy gains momentum. She’s unaware of Bing Bong’s absence.)

speaker: Joy
text: We’re gonna make it!
parenthetical: (The rocket makes it over the ledge! It crashes atop the cliff.)

speaker: Joy
text: Woo-hoo! Bing Bong, we did it! We--
parenthetical: (She turns around. She’s alone.)

speaker: Joy
text: Bing Bong? Bing Bong!
parenthetical: (She runs to the cliff side. Below, Bing Bong laughs excitedly, happy to help Riley in the only way he can.)

speaker: BING BONG
text: Ya ha ha! You made it! Ha ha! Go! Go save Riley! Take her to the moon for me. Okay?
parenthetical: (He waves as the last of him vanishes into the air. Joy’s eyes widen in awe; he’s sacrificed his life for her.)

speaker: Joy
text: I'll try, Bing Bong. I promise.
parenthetical: (Joy gets up and soldiers on.)

speaker: MOM
text: We’re home! Riley? Riley!
parenthetical: (They check in Riley's room, but she's not here.)

speaker: MOM
text: I’ll call her cell.
parenthetical: (Riley walks through a sketchy part of the city.)

speaker: FEAR
text: This is terrible. Wait. Is that someone walking towards us? Let’s cross the street.
parenthetical: (Riley’s cell phone rings. MOM is calling.)

speaker: FEAR
text: It's Mom! She's on to us! Where's my bag?
parenthetical: (Fear finds a paper bag and breathes into it to calm himself.)

speaker: DISGUST
text: What do we do?

speaker: ANGER
text: Riley needs to get core memories. We keep going.
parenthetical: (Riley ignores the call and walks on.) | (Joy runs through the Long-Term Memory stacks.)

speaker: JOY
text: Sadness!
parenthetical: (She sees Family Island rumble, pieces beginning to break off. She frantically continues on.) | (Riley walks into the bus depot.) | (Joy runs through a long corridor.)

speaker: JOY
text: Sadness? Sadness? C’mon Sadness, where are you? Okay. If I were Sadness, where would I be?
parenthetical: (Joy slumps to the ground. She kicks a leg up in the air.)

speaker: JOY
text: Ohhh... everything is awful and my legs don’t work and you have to drag me around while I touch all the--
parenthetical: (Joy stops. Something has caught her eye. REVEAL: a path of blue memories on the bottom shelves. Joy follows the path.) | (Riley walks out of the bus depot and into the advancing line. Her cell phone rings again.) | [CONT'D]

speaker: DISGUST
text: Oh, no. It’s Mom again. What do we do?

speaker: ANGER
text: Uh...
parenthetical: (Boom! Family Island rumbles behind them. QUICK INTER-CUTS between the line getting shorter, Riley declining Mom’s call, and Family Island crumbling. Finally... .)

speaker: ANGER
text: This is madness! She shouldn’t run away!

speaker: DISGUST
text: Let’s get this idea out of her head.
parenthetical: (They rush to unplug the idea.) | (Joy runs, following the path of blue memories.)

speaker: JOY
text: Sadness?
parenthetical: (Joy rounds a corner and spots Sadness far down the next row.)

speaker: JOY
text: Sadness!
parenthetical: (Sadness turns around.) | [CONT’D]

speaker: SADNESS
text: Joy?
parenthetical: (Inexplicably, Sadness turns and runs away.)

speaker: JOY
text: Wait, Sadness!
parenthetical: [chases after her.]

speaker: SADNESS
text: Just let me go! Riley’s better off without me!
parenthetical: (Joy follows Sadness straight into Imagination land. Sadness topples giant french fries in Joy’s path.)

speaker: JOY
text: Come back!
parenthetical: (Joy uses a super-long fry to vault over the mound of fries.) | (In Cloud Town, two MIND WORKER COPS are questioning a CLOUD WITNESS.)

speaker: CLOUD WITNESS
text: Yes! And there she is!
parenthetical: (Joy runs right through the Cloud Witness, dissipating her.)

speaker: JOY
text: What? Sadness!

speaker: SADNESS
text: I only make everything worse!

speaker: JOY
text: Wait-- Sadness! We gotta get you back to... Ugh.
parenthetical: (Joy looks back at Family Island crumbling. She looks around: what does she have to work with? The Boyfriend Generator...) | (Anger tries to untwist the Idea Bulb, but IT WON’T MOVE.)

speaker: ANGER
text: It’s stuck!

speaker: DISGUST
text: Oh, great.

speaker: FEAR
text: What? What do you mean it’s stuck?!

speaker: DISGUST
text: Now what?
parenthetical: (A strange BLACKNESS spreads over the console.)

speaker: FEAR
text: Oh, no, no, no, no, no, no. What is THIS?!
parenthetical: (Anger slams a chair down on the console. It bounces off with no effect. Fear lunges in with a crowbar. He loses control and it smacks him in the face.) | (Riley walks down the aisle of the bus to take a seat.)

speaker: FEAR
text: How do we stop it?

speaker: DISGUST
text: I got it! Make her feel scared! That’ll make her change her mind!

speaker: FEAR
text: Brilliant!

speaker: DISGUST
text: I know it’s brilliant. Do it!
parenthetical: (Fear frantically pushes buttons.)

speaker: FEAR
text: Ahh! Nothing’s working! Wh-Why isn’t it working??

speaker: ANGER
text: Let me try!
parenthetical: (Disgust and Fear pound on the console buttons.)

speaker: DISGUST
text: Great, you broke it!

speaker: FEAR
text: No, I didn’t!

speaker: ANGER
text: Let me do it!
parenthetical: (By now, the console is mostly black.)

speaker: FEAR
text: Guys..... we can’t make Riley feel anything.

speaker: ANGER
text: What have we done?
parenthetical: (Joy runs up to Riley’s Imaginary Boyfriend.)

speaker: JOY
text: Hey! Hey, look at me. Did you mean what you said before?

speaker: IMAGINARY BOYFRIEND
text: I would die for Riley! I would die for Riley!

speaker: JOY
text: Yeah, yeah, okay, Haircut. Time to prove it.
parenthetical: (Joy scoops him into her satchel. She activates the generator. Imaginary Boyfriends pour out, directly into Joy’s satchel.) | (Joy spots Sadness ahead, and runs past her.) | (She pulls a balloon free from a TWISTY BALLOON PALM TREE. She aims it towards the approaching Sadness and unties it. PTHHPT! The balloon blows Sadness back over the MEMORY DUMP. In the process, the HOUSE OF CARDS behind them collapses.)

speaker: HOUSE OF CARDS WORKER
text: That’s it! I fold! JOY This is crazy, this is crazy... No, no, no! Joy! Be positive. I am POSITIVE this is crazy!
parenthetical: (Joy sprints by grabbing the SATCHEL, now full of boyfriends.) | (Sadness floats out over the Memory Dump. Joy runs along the cliff edge, parallel to Sadness, towards Family Island. Joy sights Sadness and aligns herself with Family Island. She dumps the satchel, pouring out the Boyfriends. Their momentum pulls her to the top of a TOWER OF BOYFRIENDS.) | (Joy spots the trampoline on Family Island, then Sadness floating away. She times it...)

speaker: JOY
text: NOW!
parenthetical: (The tower tips forward.) | [CONT’D]

speaker: IMAGINARY BOYFRIENDS
text: FOR RILEY!!!
parenthetical: (Joy falls towards Family Island, bounces on the TRAMPOLINE, flying up towards Sadness. She intercepts Sadness midair...)

speaker: SADNESS
text: Joy?

speaker: JOY
text: Hang on!
parenthetical: (They launch towards Headquarters. Will they make it?) | (Anger, Fear and Disgust watch the screen, worried.)

speaker: FEAR
text: Oh, I wish Joy was here!
parenthetical: (SPLAT! Joy and Sadness hit the back window and slide down, grabbing the window edge. The Emotions run to the window.)

speaker: DISGUST
text: It’s Joy!

speaker: ANGER
text: Stand back! Arrrrgh!
parenthetical: (Anger throws a chair at the window. It bounces off.)

speaker: DISGUST
text: That worked.

speaker: ANGER
text: Well, what would you do, if you’re so smart?!
parenthetical: (Anger's head is igniting; this gives Disgust an idea.)

speaker: Disgust
text: I'd tell you, but you're too dumb to understand.

speaker: Anger
text: WHAT?!

speaker: Disgust
text: Of course, your tiny brain is confused. Guess I'll just have to dumb it down to your level. Sorry I don't speak moron as well as you. But let me try. Duhhhhhh!

speaker: Anger
text: AAAAAAAGGGGGGGGGHHHHHHHHHHH!!!!!!!!!!
parenthetical: (And what a yell and fire it is. Disgust puts on a welding mask and aims Anger at the window, breaking a hole in the glass. Once Anger has calmed down, Disgust and Fear help Joy and Sadness inside safely.)

speaker: Fear
text: Oh, thank goodness you're back!

speaker: Disgust
text: Things are really messed up. You gotta... the controls, Joy!

speaker: Anger
text: We-we found this idea and now Riley’s on a bus heading for Minnesota!

speaker: Fear
text: We were trying to make happy memories!
parenthetical: (On the screen, Riley stares out the bus window. The bus is pulling out; nearby the depot is the highway leading to Bay Bridge, which leads out of San Francisco with no turning back.)

speaker: Disgust
text: Joy, you got to fix this. Get out there.

speaker: Joy
text: Sadness, it's up to you.

speaker: Sadness
text: Me? All: Sadness?!

speaker: Sadness
text: I can't, Joy.

speaker: Joy
text: Yes, you can. Riley needs you.
parenthetical: (Sadness is surprised; Joy nods in affirmation The bus is exiting the parking lot; Realizing her true purpose, Sadness walks over to the console. The console is almost completely black.)

speaker: Sadness
text: Okay...
parenthetical: (Resisting a bit, she puts her hands on the bulb; the other emotions watch with worry and anticipation. All of the controls stop working and the console is completely black now. The bus pulls out onto the road into the direction of the highway. Sadness then does her thing and twists the bulb, successfully unscrewing it and turning it off. The console's blackness retracts, reactivating the controls, and the bulb rises and pops out.) | (Outside, feeling comes back to Riley as she realizes what she is doing. She gets out of her seat.)

speaker: Riley
text: Wait, stop! I wanna get off!
parenthetical: (The bus stops just before the highway entrance and Riley jumps off and runs away. Joy and the emotions are surprised; Riley continues running down the sidewalk back home.)

speaker: Mrs. Andersen
text: Alright, thank you, we will. Her teacher hasn't even seen Riley all day!

speaker: DAD
text: What? I can't believe this.

speaker: MOM
text: What was she wearing last? Do you even remember what--
parenthetical: (The door opens. Riley walks in. They rush over to her.)

speaker: MOM
text: Riley!

speaker: DAD
text: Riley, there you are! Thank goodness!

speaker: MOM
text: Oh, we were worried sick! Where have you been? It’s so late... (In Headquarters, Joy decides it's time for Sadness to do her stuff. Without hesitation, she gives her the Core Memories; Sadness scoops them up, and they all turn blue. One by one, Sadness places each memory in the recall unit. Then, she placed her hand on the console. It turns BLUE. For the first time, Riley cries.]
parenthetical: (Riley, what happened? Are you alright?)

speaker: Riley
text: I know you don't want me to, but, I miss home. I miss Minnesota. You need me to be happy. But, I want my old friends and my hockey team. I wanna go home. Please don't be mad.

speaker: Mom
text: Oh, sweetie.

speaker: Dad
text: We're not mad. You know what? I miss Minnesota too. I miss the woods where we took hikes.

speaker: Mom
text: And the backyard where you used to play.

speaker: Dad
text: The Spring Lake, where you learned to skate. Come here.

speaker: Fear
text: Hey, I'm liking this new view.

speaker: Anger
text: Friendship Island has expanded. Glad they finally opened that Friendly Argument section.

speaker: Sadness
text: I like Tragic Vampire Romance Island.

speaker: Disgust
text: Fashion Island. Everyone shut up! Fear Boy Band Island. Hope that's just a phase.

speaker: Joy
text: Say what you want, I think it’s all beautiful.

speaker: WORKER FRITZ
text: Alright!
parenthetical: (The Emotions turn to see workers at a new, larger console which is later used for Inside Out 2.)

speaker: WORKER FRITZ
text: There you go. Your new expanded console is up and running.
parenthetical: (The new console lights up.) | [CONT’D]

speaker: Emotions
text: Whoa!

speaker: DISGUST
text: Cool. Upgrade!

speaker: JOY
text: Ooohh.

speaker: Fear
text: Hey. Whoa. FEAR Whoops, wait. Did I just do that?
parenthetical: (Fear leans on the console.) | (BEEP!)

speaker: DISGUST
text: Hey guys? What’s “pub-er-ty?”

speaker: JOY
text: I don’t know. It’s probably not important.

speaker: ANGER
text: Whoa. I have access to the entire Curse Word Library! This new console is the--
parenthetical: (BEEP!)

speaker: FEAR
text: Sorry. I did it again. My bad.

speaker: DISGUST
text: They’re getting to the rink!

speaker: DAD
text: Now when you get out there, you be aggressive!

speaker: RILEY
text: I know, Dad.

speaker: RILEY
text: You know, you guys don’t have to come to every game.
parenthetical: [Mom and Dad’s faces are painted blue and green.]

speaker: DAD
text: Are you kidding? I’m not missing one! GO FOGHORNS!

speaker: MOM
text: GO RILEY! FOGHORNS ARE THE BEST! RILEY Okay, okay. I gotta go. JOY Awww...
parenthetical: [Riley walks away, mortified, as Dad makes foghorn noises.]

speaker: FEAR
text: They love us!

speaker: ANGER
text: Yeah, Mom and Dad are pretty cool.

speaker: DISGUST
text: Guys, of course they are. But we can’t SHOW IT! DAD’S FEAR: She loved the face painting! DAD’S ANGER: Ha! Told you it was a great idea! MOM’S ANGER: Best idea he’s had in awhile. MOM’S SADNESS: He’s a really good guy. MOM’S FEAR: Just in case.
parenthetical: [Zoom in to Dad’s head... Dad’s Emotions cheer. They all have their faces painted. ON THE SCREEN: Mom beams at Dad.] | [Dad turns to Mom. She smiles, and we zoom in to her head... All Mom’s Emotions are also painted.] | [They all sigh. Mom’s Anger tosses the MEMORY of the Brazilian helicopter pilot. Mom’s Fear retrieves it.] | [Riley walks towards the ice and bumps into A TEEN BOY. He drops his water bottle. She picks it up for him.]

speaker: RILEY
text: Oh. Sorry!
parenthetical: [She smiles. In the boy's mind, his Emotions run around in a panic, screaming while a warning sign flashed. One of them is HUDDLED, crying. Outside, the boy stands stunned, unable to speak. Riley hands him the water bottle.]

speaker: RILEY
text: Uhh... Ooooo-kay. Bye!
parenthetical: (Riley skates out onto the ice, looking back over her shoulder... with a hint of a smile?)

speaker: JOY
text: You ready?

speaker: SADNESS
text: Yeah!

speaker: JOY
text: Alright. Let’s play some hockey! JOY All right Anger, take it!
parenthetical: (Riley faces off. The puck drops.)

speaker: ANGER
text: Give us that puck or you’re dead meat!
parenthetical: (Riley gets it!) | [O.S.]

speaker: FEAR
text: On our left! On our left!
parenthetical: (An opponent steals the puck.) | [O.S.]

speaker: DISGUST
text: Let’s just try not to get all smelly this time.

speaker: SADNESS
text: Ooh, Mom and Dad are watching us fail.

speaker: ANGER
text: Not for long!
parenthetical: (Riley regains the puck and skates off with a smile.) | [O.S.]

speaker: JOY
text: We’ve been through a lot lately, that’s for sure. But we still love our girl. She has great new friends, a great new house... things couldn’t be better! After all, Riley’s 12 now. What could happen? Mid Credits Mid Credits Scene

speaker: Teacher
text: And who can tell me what year San Francisco was founded? Anyone? Jordan.

speaker: Teacher's Disgust
text: How much more of this?

speaker: Teacher's Sadness
text: Five months, two weeks and four days until summer vacation.

speaker: Teacher's Joy
text: Then it's off to the Bahamas with you know who.
parenthetical: (As Teacher's Sadness, Anger, Fear and Disgust go to the console, Teacher's Joy presses a button and summons a yellow memory orb of the Brazilian Helicopter Pilot.)

speaker: Brazilian Helicopter Pilot
text: Come, fly with me, gatinha.
parenthetical: (All five of Teacher's emotions sigh dreamingly.)

speaker: Pizza Girl
text: Have a nice day.

speaker: Pizza Girl's Disgust
text: What are we mad about?

speaker: Pizza Girl's Anger
text: I dunno, whatever Troy is mad about.

speaker: Pizza Girl's Disgust
text: What's Troy mad about?

speaker: Pizza Girl's Anger
text: I dunno.

speaker: Pizza Girl's Joy
text: I dunno

speaker: Pizza Girl's Fear
text: I dunno.

speaker: Cool Girl
text: I know. Totally!

speaker: Cool Girl's Fear
text: We're a total fraud. Do you think they can see through us?

speaker: Cool Girl's Anger
text: Of course not, we're wearing eye shadow!

speaker: Cool Girl's Sadness
text: Being cool is so exhausting!

speaker: Jangles
text: Who's the birthday girl, huh? Hey! ... Hello?

speaker: Jangles' Joy
text: 6 years of drama school for this?
parenthetical: (Bus Driver's Multiple Anger's complain about the traffic)

speaker: Bus Driver's Red Anger
text: You know what? This is OK. This is fine. We just need to go to our happy place.
parenthetical: (As the Bus Driver's Red Anger presses the button, what they got actually is the memory of the Triple Dent Gum commercial and they furiously yelled about it. Then a man is seen trying to eat a pizza that the dog smells it.)
